profiles.combinedText:
``` ```
> 3 Hours Passive Growth Time
> 2000 Combat Weight

**Lifestyle:**
    Terrestrial

**Group Limit:** 
     5 adults, 5 offspring (below sub-adult)

**Herd Tolerance:**
> Albertaceratops, Amargasaurus, Camptosaurus, Citipati, Deinocheirus, Eotriceratops, Garrigatitan, Pachyrhinosaurus, Pentaceratops, Struthiomimus, and Styracosaurus.
> *Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.*

**Mandatory Behavior:** 
○  __Gorger__: Pachycephalosaurus may claim any berry bush they are eating from.  If a low-tier or above herbivore not in their group starts eating from the bush, Pachycephalosaurus may be vocally aggressive. After 3 threat calls if the other herbivores do not attempt to flee from the berry bush, Pachycephalosaurus may become combat aggressive until the herbivore flees from the berry bush.
○ __Territorial__: When in a **Pachycephalosaurus-only** herd's claimed territory, **adult** Pachycephalosaurus must be vocally aggressive to other **adult** Pachycephalosaurus not in their group.  If after 3 threat calls, the herbivore(s) do not attempt to flee, **adult** Pachycephalosaurus may become **combat aggressive** and chase the herbivore(s) out of the territory. If *fearful or unwilling* to engage other **adult** Pachycephalosaurus, they **must** instead  **immediately surrender** and **leave** the territory.
○ __Fearful Abandonment__: Pachycephalosaurus **must not** defend grouped adolescent and below herbivores from adult sub-apex carnivore packs, or adult apex carnivores.
-

**Carnivore Aggression:** 
- When **solo** or at **2 adults**, Pachycephalosaurus **must** be **vocally aggressive** to all **solo** adult low-tier carnivores. If after 3 threat calls, the carnivores do not attempt to flee, Pachycephalosaurus *may* become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee the territory or perish. 
- When **adolescent and below** are present, adult Pachycephalosaurus **must** be **vocally aggressive** to all adult mid-tier and below carnivores. If after 3 threat calls, the carnivores do not attempt to flee, Pachycephalosaurus *may* become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee the territory or perish.
- When in a **same-species herd** with 3 or more adults present, adult Pachycephalosaurus **must** be **vocally aggressive** to all *approaching* **solo** adult sub-apex carnivores and adult mid-tier carnivore **groups**. If after 3 threat calls, the carnivores do not attempt to flee from Pachycephalosaurus and its herd, Pachycephalosaurus *may* become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee the territory or perish.
- When in a **mixed herd** with adult sub-apex and above herbivores present, adult Pachycephalosaurus **must** be **vocally aggressive** to all *approaching* solo adult sub-apex carnivores. If after 3 threat calls, the carnivores do not attempt to flee from Pachycephalosaurus and its herd, Pachycephalosaurus *may* become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee the territory or perish.
-

**Scavenger Aggression:** 
- Adult Pachycephalosaurus **must be** vocally aggressive to scavenger **groups**. If after 3 threat calls the scavengers do not attempt to flee away from the Pachycephalosaurus or its herd, they may become combat aggressive until the scavengers flee the territory or perish.

**Scavenger High Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, they must be **bothered** and must immediately shake-off and **initiate combat** on the latched scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they **must not** fight back and instead **must flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers **must not** initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*

**Territory Size:** 
> Small - Needs at least 4 adults to claim.

**Leadership Type:** 
  Patriarchal - Male Dominant. 
-

**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season__: Dry
- __Preferred Nesting Location(s)__: 
> **Gondwa**: Desolate Pass, Hoodoo Expanse, Sand Caverns
> **Panjura**: Lakeside Meadow, Little Brook Meadow, Little Sand Lake 
> **Riparia**: Dry Fang Canyon, Cedrus Forest, East Passage
- __Preferred Skins/Coloration(s)__: Both male and female Pachycephalosaurus would typically have neutral tones on their bodies and faces throughout the year, though patches of color, on occasion, were not uncommon.  However, males would have a vibrant dome color that was unlike the rest of their body. Younger males would often have more vibrant colors and, as they would age, the color would soften.
> Pachycephalosaurus would go through a plethora of rituals during nesting seasons.  Known for their energy and vigor, males would compete amongst one another to show their strength and endurance especially with the harsh environments they’d reside in.  At times, males would quite literally butt heads, oftening fighting against one another by ramming their domes in an attempt to overpower one another.  This approach was only common with hot-headed and younger males, while older males would prefer to do courting dances with their mate, not being as energetic as they once were.  The older males were also more emotionally in-tune with their mate.  Both males and females would take turns foraging for food and teaching their young how to use their dome to their advantage when foraging.  Hatchlings and juveniles were typically very rowdy, though the runts of the litter were more docile.  Female’s would tend to the runts more closely, becoming very protective of them and making sure they got to eat first.
-

**Optional Behavior:** 
  When it comes to who to follow, the most dominant male is chosen by sparring contests.  Females, although not the most caring parents, will keep their young close until they reach sub-adult.  They are social, often being vocal to communicate with the herd.  Males frequently spar, often to decide dominance or sometimes even over food. 
``` ```

profiles.combinedText:
``` ```
> 4 Hours Passive Growth Time
> 5000 Combat Weight

**Lifestyle:**
    Terrestrial

**Group Limit:**
    2 adults, 3 offspring (below sub-adult)

**Herd Tolerance:**
> Citipati, Gigantoraptor, Halszkaraptor, Psittacosaurus, Struthiomimus, and Suzhousaurus.
> *Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.*

**Mandatory Behavior:**
○ __Survivor:__ **Adolescent and below** Therizinosaurus **must flee** from **adult mid tier and above** carnivores. May defend themself if cornered or exhausted.
○ __Territorial:__  When in their **claimed territory**, adult Therizinosaurus **must** be vocally aggressive towards sub-apex and below herbivores not in their group, and other adult Therizinosaurus not in their group within line of sight. If after 3 threat calls the herbviores do not attempt to flee, Therizinosaurus *may* be **combat aggressive** until the herbivores flees the territory or perish.
○ __Aquaphobic:__ **Adult** Therizinosaurus **must not** enter the water once combat has started.

**Carnivore Aggression:**
- When **solo** or when **adolescent or below herbivores are present**, adult Therizinosaurus must be **vocally aggressive** towards all approaching adult carnivores. If after 3 threat calls, the carnivore do not attempt to flee from Therizinosaurus and its herd, Therizinosaurus *may* become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee the territory or perish.
- When in an **adult only herd**, Therizinosaurus is **unbothered** by mid-tier and below carnivores. If combat is initated by the carnivore, Therizinosaurus may defend themself and their herd until the carnivores flee. 
- When in an **adult only herd**, Therizinosaurus **must** be **vocally aggressive** towards adult sub-apex and adult apex carnivores. If after 3 threat calls, the carnivore do not attempt to flee from Therizinosaurus and its herd, Therizinosaurus *may* become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee the territory or perish.
-

**Scavenger Aggression:**
- When adolescent and below Therizinosaurus present, adult Therizinosaurus **must** be v**ocally aggressive** towards scavengers. If after 3 threat calls, the scavengers do not attempt to flee from Therizinosaurus and its herd, Therizinosaurus may become combat aggressive until the carnivores flee the territory or perish.
- When in an adult-only herd, Therizinosaurus is **unbothered** by scavengers.

**Scavenger Moderate Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, may be unbothered. If bothered by the latched on scavengers, must threat call **two** times or shake **two** times before attacking the scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they must **not fight** back and instead must **flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers cannot attack/initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*

**Territory Size:**
> Medium- Can claim as a solo adult or Therizinosaurus group. 
> Large - Needs 2 adult Therizinosaurus members to claim. 
> [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing)

**Leadership Type:**
   Mated Pairs - Dual Leadership.
-

**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season__: Dry
- __Preferred Nesting Location(s)__: 
> **Gondwa**: Dark Woods, Hunter’s Thicket, Titan’s Pass
> **Panjura**: Aperture Pass, Dark Wood Haven, Threehorn Grassland 
> **Riparia:** Dry Fang Canyon, Hollow Hills, Wind Tunnels
- __Preferred Skins/Coloration(s)__: Female Therizinosaurs would often display brighter colors along their tail feathers and often had dark colors transition to white at the tips.  Males, on the opposite end, would have brightly saturated crest feathers while the rest of their body would remain the same or similar shade. 
> Female Therizinosaurus were known to be far more territorial and aggressive than males.  They would infrequently find suitable male mates which resulted in duo mares often courting each other.  Though aggressive to males, females would exhibit very caring and emotionally in-tuned behaviors.  Acts of preening and sharing meals with one another were ways that these pairs could bond.  Though they couldn’t lay eggs, female pairs would often adopt orphaned Therizinosaurus and were also known to adopt Citipati and Struthiomimus young, treating them no differently than their own kin.  
-

**Optional Behavior:**
If confronted and threatened by a carnivore, Therizinosaurus will not back down easily and tends to stand its ground even against threatening Apex carnivores.  When grouped with a mate and offspring, Therizinosaurus is often seen taking "shifts" between patrolling the territory and watching over the offspring.  Therizinosaurus will also choose to rest and preside over their food sources within their territory, especially when with offspring.  They often graze on their claimed food sources, keeping an eye out for potential carnivores that may prey on their young or even herbivores that may aim to consume all their food.  Therizinosaurus tends to be most active from dawn-to-dusk, choosing not to roam or migrate once night-time arrives. 
``` ```

profiles.combinedText:
``` ```
> 1 Hour Passive Growth Time
> 1600 Combat Weight

**Lifestyle:**
    Terrestrial

**Group Limit:**
    Unlimited

**Herd Tolerance:**
> Amargasaurus, Anodontosaurus, Apatosaurus, Barsboldia, Bonapartenykus, Citipati, Deinocheirus, Edmontosaurus Annectens, Edmontosaurus Regalis, Eotriceratops, Garrigatitan, Halszkaraptor, Iguanodon, Kentrosaurus, Lambeosaurus, Lurdusaurus, Miragaia, Muttaburrasaurus, Nasutoceratops, Pachycephalosaurs, Parasaurolophus, Stegosaurus, Struthiomimus, Styracosaurus, Tenontosaurus,  and Yunnanosaurus. 
> *Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.*

**Mandatory Behavior: **
○ __Safety in Numbers__: When in a **same-species herd** of **3 or less adults**, Camptosaurus are **fearful of** and **must flee** from mid-tier and above carnivores. May defend themself if cornered or exhausted.
○ __On Alert:__ **Solo** adult Camptosaurus may use alert  (<:AlertCall:1114671294009581569>) or broadcast ( <:Broadcast_Call:1395945878275227732> ) calls to warn other herbivores of approaching carnivores. 
- *Note: Camptosaurus **must** remain separated from any herbivores they are not grouped with, and **must not** third-party in any way (see [Combat Rule 8](https://docs.google.com/document/d/15y-5x9DatxAUlJFKO4S2qMpN1VhAZrtkbpZYtYIs1h0/edit?tab=t.0))*
○ __Survivor__: **Adolescent and below** Camptosaurus **must flee** from adult scavenger tier and above carnivores. May defend themself if cornered or exhausted.

**Carnivore Aggression:**
- When **solo**, or in a **same species herd**, adult Camptosaurus are **wary** of adult low-tier and above carnivores.
- When in a **mix-herd** with **3 or more** adults, adult Camptosaurus may be **vocally aggressive** to adult low-tier carnivores. If after **3 threat calls**, the carnivores do not attempt to flee from Camptosaurus or its herd, Camptosaurus *may* become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee the territory or perish.

**Scavenger Aggression: **
- Adult Camptosaurus *may* be **vocally aggressive** to all scavengers. If after **3 threat calls**, the scavengers do not attempt to flee from Camptosaurus or its herd, Camptosaurus *may* become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee the territory or perish.

**Scavenger High Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, they must be **bothered** and must immediately shake-off and **initiate combat** on the latched scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they **must not** fight back and instead **must flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers **must not** initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*

**Territory Size:**
> Nomadic, cannot claim territories when in a Camptosaurus only herd. [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing)

**Leadership Type: **
  Swarm Mind - Mimic Behavior/Group Thought. 
-

**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season:__ Dry
- __Preferred Nesting Location(s):__ 
> **Gondwa**: Dried Lake, Green Hills, Triad Falls 
> **Panjura**: Lakeside Meadow, Plateau Hill Haven, Redhills Haven
> **Riparia**: Dry Fang Canyon, Wind Tunnels, Twisted Forest
- __Preferred Skins/Coloration(s):__ Both male and female Camptosaurus preferred to match their colorations with their environment, choosing shades of browns and greens to camouflage in plain sight.
> Camptosaurus, while lacking in combat knowledge, served as an alert system when adopted into larger herbivorous herds.  On their own, they were gentle and soft-mannered with herd mates.  Pairs would attempt to mate for life and grow strong bonds, however if there was a need to save themselves from a predator, they wouldn’t turn back if their mate was in danger knowing that they would likely both perish in combat.  This same behavior translated into their parenting behavior.  They were deeply caring of their young and would take great time and care into making nests for their juveniles, however, if faced with a threat they would not try to defend their young and instead chose to flee for their lives instead.  Most Camptosaurus young had an innate ability to hide in plain sight, like their guardians, but others may not have been so fortunate.

**Optional Behavior:** 
Will seek dense undergrowth and forests to live in.  Will stick with its group when traveling, but will leave anyone behind when in danger.  Most active at dawn and noon.  It’s a very social and vocal  dinosaur and can be seen hanging around near other herbivores and try to get itself into a herd if given the chance. 
``` ```

**Herd Tolerance:**
> Amargasaurus, Anodontosaurus, Apatosaurus, Barsboldia, Bonapartenykus, Citipati, Deinocheirus, Edmontosaurus Annectens, Edmontosaurus Regalis, Eotriceratops, Garrigatitan, Halszkaraptor, Iguanodon, Kentrosaurus, Lambeosaurus, Lurdusaurus, Miragaia, Muttaburrasaurus, Nasutoceratops, Pachycephalosaurs, Parasaurolophus, Stegosaurus, Struthiomimus, Styracosaurus, Tenontosaurus,  and Yunnanosaurus. 
> *Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.*

**Mandatory Behavior: **
○ __Safety in Numbers__: When in a **same-species herd** of **3 or less adults**, Camptosaurus are **fearful of** and **must flee** from mid-tier and above carnivores. May defend themself if cornered or exhausted.
○ __On Alert:__ **Solo** adult Camptosaurus may use alert  (<:AlertCall:1114671294009581569>) or broadcast ( <:Broadcast_Call:1395945878275227732> ) calls to warn other herbivores of approaching carnivores. 
- *Note: Camptosaurus **must** remain separated from any herbivores they are not grouped with, and **must not** third-party in any way (see [Combat Rule 8](https://docs.google.com/document/d/15y-5x9DatxAUlJFKO4S2qMpN1VhAZrtkbpZYtYIs1h0/edit?tab=t.0))*
○ __Survivor__: **Adolescent and below** Camptosaurus **must flee** from adult scavenger tier and above carnivores. May defend themself if cornered or exhausted.

**Carnivore Aggression:**
- When **solo**, or in a **same species herd**, adult Camptosaurus are **wary** of adult low-tier and above carnivores.
- When in a **mix-herd** with **3 or more** adults, adult Camptosaurus may be **vocally aggressive** to adult low-tier carnivores. If after **3 threat calls**, the carnivores do not attempt to flee from Camptosaurus or its herd, Camptosaurus *may* become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee the territory or perish.

**Scavenger Aggression: **
- Adult Camptosaurus *may* be **vocally aggressive** to all scavengers. If after **3 threat calls**, the scavengers do not attempt to flee from Camptosaurus or its herd, Camptosaurus *may* become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee the territory or perish.

**Scavenger High Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, they must be **bothered** and must immediately shake-off and **initiate combat** on the latched scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they **must not** fight back and instead **must flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers **must not** initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*

**Territory Size:**
> Nomadic, cannot claim territories when in a Camptosaurus only herd. [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing)

**Leadership Type: **
  Swarm Mind - Mimic Behavior/Group Thought. 
-

rawMessages.author: frostwinterwolf
rawMessages.content:
**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season:__ Dry
- __Preferred Nesting Location(s):__ 
> **Gondwa**: Dried Lake, Green Hills, Triad Falls 
> **Panjura**: Lakeside Meadow, Plateau Hill Haven, Redhills Haven
> **Riparia**: Dry Fang Canyon, Wind Tunnels, Twisted Forest
- __Preferred Skins/Coloration(s):__ Both male and female Camptosaurus preferred to match their colorations with their environment, choosing shades of browns and greens to camouflage in plain sight.
> Camptosaurus, while lacking in combat knowledge, served as an alert system when adopted into larger herbivorous herds.  On their own, they were gentle and soft-mannered with herd mates.  Pairs would attempt to mate for life and grow strong bonds, however if there was a need to save themselves from a predator, they wouldn’t turn back if their mate was in danger knowing that they would likely both perish in combat.  This same behavior translated into their parenting behavior.  They were deeply caring of their young and would take great time and care into making nests for their juveniles, however, if faced with a threat they would not try to defend their young and instead chose to flee for their lives instead.  Most Camptosaurus young had an innate ability to hide in plain sight, like their guardians, but others may not have been so fortunate.

**Optional Behavior:** 
Will seek dense undergrowth and forests to live in.  Will stick with its group when traveling, but will leave anyone behind when in danger.  Most active at dawn and noon.  It’s a very social and vocal  dinosaur and can be seen hanging around near other herbivores and try to get itself into a herd if given the chance. 
``` ```

profiles.combinedText:
``` ```
> 2 Hours Passive Growth Time
> 2400 Combat Weight

**Group Limit:**
  5 adults, 5 offspring (below sub-adult)

**Herd Tolerance:**
> Apatosaurus, Argentinosaurus, Barsboldia, Camptosaurus, Citipati, Edmontosaurus Annectens, Edmontosaurus Regalis, Garrigatitan, Gigantoraptor, Iguanodon, Lambeosaurus, Lurdusaurus, Muttaburasaurus, Parasaurolophus, Pentaceratops, Styracosaurus, Struthiomimus, and Yunnanosaurus.
> *Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.*

**Mandatory Behavior:**
○ __Territorial__: When in their **claimed territory**, adult Tenontosaurus *may* be vocally aggressive towards adult low-tier and mid-tier herbivores not in their group. If after 3 threat calls the herbivores do not attempt to flee the Tenontosaurus and its herd, the Tenontosaurus *may* become combat aggressive until the other hebivore flees the territory or perish.
○ __Stand your Ground__: When **adolescent and below** Hadrosaurs are present, Tenontosaurus **must not** pursue fleeing dinosaurs.
- Note: Hadrosaurs include: Barsboldia, Parasaurolophus, Lambeosaurus, Iguanodon, Muttaburrasaurus, Tenontosaurus, Edmontosaurus Regalis, Edmontosaurus Annectes.
○ __Survivor__: Adolescent and below Tenontosaurus **must flee** from adult mid-tier and above carnivores. May defend themself if cornered or exhausted.

**Carnivore Aggression:**
- Tenontosaurus are **fearful** of and **must flee** from adult apex carnivores. May defend themself if cornered or exhausted. Tenontosaurus **must not** pursue fleeing apex carnivores.
- When **solo**, Tenontosaurus *may* be vocally aggressive to approaching *solo* **low-tier** carnivores.  If after 3 threat calls the carnivore does not attempt flee, Tentontosaurus may become **combat aggressive** until the carnivore flees the territory or perish.
- When in a **adult-only hadrosaur herd**, Tenontosaurus *may* be vocally aggressive to *approaching* low-tier and mid-tier carnivores.  If after 3 threat calls the carnivore do not attempt to flee from Tenontosaurus or its herd, they *may* become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee the territory or perish.
- *Note: Hadrosaurs include: Barsboldia, Parasaurolophus, Lambeosaurus, Iguanodon, Muttaburrasaurus, Tenontosaurus, Edmontosaurus Regalis, Edmontosaurus Annectes.*
-

**Scavenger Aggression:**
- *Must* be vocally aggressive to scavengers in the Tenontosaurus' personal space. If after 3 threat calls the scavengers do not attempt to flee from Tenontosaurus, they *may* become **combat aggressive** until the scavengers flee.
- When **adolescent and below**  Tenontosaurus are present,  adult Tenontosaurus **must** be vocally aggressive towards scavengers. If after 3 threat calls and/or mock attacks the scavengers do not attempt to flee from Tenontosaurus or its herd, they *may* become **combat aggressive** until the scavengers flee.

**Scavenger High Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, **must** be **bothered** and **must** immediately shake-off and **initiate combat** on the latched scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they **must not fight** back and instead **must flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers cannot attack/initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*

**Territory Size:**
> Small - need 3 adult members to claim.
> Medium - need 5 adults needed to claim.
> [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing)

**Leadership Type:**
    Patriarchal - Male Dominant

**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season:__ Dry
- __Preferred Nesting Location(s):__ 
> **Gondwa**: Broken Tooth Canyon, Ripple Beach, Stego Mountains 
> **Panjura**: Hilltop Haven Pond, The Cascades, World's Edge Falls 
> **Riparia**: Big Tree Overlook, Dry Fang Canyon, Wollemi Forest
- __Preferred Skins/Coloration(s):__ Leading males would have bright colors and, at times, they would have distinct white tail markings distinguishing them from the rest of the herd.  Females would prefer to be neutral colors, commonly browns or greens, but, if partnered with a leading male, a chosen female would also show off similar white markings.

**Optional Behavior:**
> Tenontosaurus were highly adaptable creatures and were able to live in harsh, arid ecosystems, unlike other herbivore species.  They often traveled together in large herds, though living in dry environments they would prefer to nest in places with shallow water sources to give young places to drink and to exercise swimming.  Juvenile Tenonto would frequently play-fight, males being rougher than females, in an attempt to impress leading males and be viewed as more capable.  Leading males would fight amongst each other to impress females, younger males also practicing this same trait.  Young Tenontosaurus were very absent-minded and this would often lead to their downfall as they stood out in the wide open areas where their guardians would nest.  When migrating, younger Tenontosaurus were expected to keep pace as the herd would not stop if some straggled behind and these stragglers would form their own groups to protect themselves from harm's way.
``` ```

profiles.combinedText:
``` ```
> 2 Hours Passive Growth Time
> 3200 Combat Weight

**Lifestyle:**
    Semi-aquatic

**Group Limit:**
    5 adults, 6 offspring (below sub-adult)

**Herd Tolerance:**
> Amargasaurus, Ampelosaurus, Anodontosaurus, Apatosaurus, Argentinosaurus, Barsboldia, Bonapartenykus, Camptosaurus, Citipati, Deinocheirus, Edmontosaurus Annectens, Edmontosaurus Regalis, Garrigatitan, Gigantoraptor, Halszkaraptor, Iguanodon, Lambeosaurus, Muttaburrasaurus, Parasaurolophus, Struthiomimus, Tenontosaurus, and Yunnanosaurus.
> *Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.* 

**Mandatory Behavior:**
○ __Safety in Numbers__:  Lurdusaurus **must flee** adult sub-apex and above carnivores  *unless* in a group with adult sub-apex or above herbivores.
○ __Protective Parent__: When **adolescent or below** Lurdusaurus are present, adult Lurdusaurus *may* be **vocally aggressive** to all approaching mid-tier and below herbivores. If after **3 threat calls**, the mid-tier and below herbivores do not attempt to flee away from Lurdusaurus or its group, Lurdusaurus *may* become **combat aggressive** until the herbivores flee from their herd, but **must not** pursue fleeing herbivores. 
○ __Survivor__: **Adolescent and below** Lurdusaurus **must flee** from adult low tier and above carnivores. May defend themself if cornered or exhausted.

**Carnivore Aggression:**
- When **solo**, or in a **same species herd**, adult Lurdusaurus **must** be **vocally aggressive** towards all *approaching* mid-tier carnivores and below. If after **3 threat calls** the carnivores do not attempt to flee, Lurdusaurus *may* become combat aggressive until the carnivores flee the territory or perish.
- When **solo**, or in a **same species herd**, adult Lurdusaurus are **fearful of** and **must flee** from adult sub-apex carnivore groups, and adult apex and above carnivores. May defend themself if cornered or exhausted.
- When in a **mix-herd** with adult **sub-apex or above** herbivores present, adult Lurdusaurus *may* be vocally aggressive to *approaching* apex and below carnivores. If after **3 threat calls** the carnivores do not attempt to flee, Lurdusaurus *may* become combat aggressive, but **must not** pursue fleeing carnivores. 
-

**Scavenger Aggression:**
- When in an **adult-only herd**, Lurdusaurus are unbothered by scavengers
- When **adolescent and below** Lurdusaurus are present,  adult Lurdusaurus **must be** vocally aggressive towards scavenger *groups*. If after **3 threat calls**, the scavengers do not attempt flee from Lurdusaurus and its herd, Lurdusaurus **may** become combat aggressive until the scavengers flee.

**Scavenger Low Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, may be unbothered. If bothered by the latched on scavengers, they must threat call **three** times or shake **three** times before attacking the scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they **must not** fight back and instead **must flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers **must not** initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*

**Territory Size:**
> Small - Needs at least 3 adult members to claim when in a Lurdusaurus only group. 
> Medium - Needs 4 adult members to claim when in a Lurdusaurus only group.

**Leadership Type:**
    Familial - Group oriented.
-

**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season__: Wet
- __Preferred Nesting Location(s)__:
> **Gondwa:** Big Quill Lake, Broken Tooth Canyon, Whistling Columns.
> **Panjura:** Amphitheatre Lake, Bower Brook Lake, Hilltop Haven.
> **Riparia:** Stillwater Bog, Mudflats, Black Fern Hills
- __Preferred Skins/Coloration(s)__: During nesting seasons, male Lurdusaurus will often display strong blue colors to attract the eyes of a female. Females are often found displaying complex patterns that male Lurdusaurus find fixating.
> Lurdusaurus often nest in groups, using their numbers as protection against carnivores. The males patrol the nesting grounds and gather food while the females build nests and care for their young. When courting, male Lurdusaurus will gather lots of food then call out to female Lurdusaurus to gain their attention. Once a female Lurdusaurus takes notice and approves of the male, they will transport the food to her nest and remain together until their young have grown enough to sustain themselves. Hatchings are very clingy with their parents but juveniles and adolescents are much more independent.

**Optional Behavior:**
    Lurdusaurus are natural herd dinosaurs, often finding themselves alongside larger species that they use for protection. In return, Lurdusaurus will act as a scout for food for these herds. They are often found running ahead of the herd when migrating in order to search out the best possible route forward. While they are passive among other species, there is a strict familial hierarchy within their group. The eldest Lurdusaurus will always eat before the younger members. During the day Lurdusaurus will remain active, spending most of their time filling their bellies. However at night, these dinosaurs stay quiet to avoid their nocturnal predators.
``` ```

○ __Survivor__: **Adolescent and below** Lambeosaurus **must flee** from adult low tier and above carnivores. May defend themself if cornered or exhausted.

**Carnivore Aggression:**
- When **solo**, in a **same species herd**, or when **adolescent and below** herbivores are present, adult Lambeosaurus **must** be **vocally aggressive** towards all *approaching* mid-tier carnivores and below. If after **3 threat calls** the carnivores do not attempt to flee, Lambeosaurus *may* become combat aggressive until the carnivores flee the territory or perish.
- When in an **adult only mix-herd**, Lambeosaurus are **unbothered** by all carnivores.

**Scavenger Aggression: **
- When in an **adult-only herd**, Lambeosaurus are unbothered by scavengers
- When **adolescent and below** herbivores are present,  adult Lambeosaurus **must be** vocally aggressive towards scavenger *groups*. If after **3 threat calls**, the scavengers do not attempt to flee from Lambeosaurus and its herd, Lambeosaurus **may** become combat aggressive until the scavengers flee.

**Scavenger Moderate Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, may be unbothered. If bothered by the latched on scavengers, they must threat call **two** times or shake **two** times before attacking the scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they **must not** fight back and instead **must flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers **must not** initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*
-

**Territory Size:**
> Small - Needs at least 3 adult members to claim.  
> Medium - Needs at least 4 adult members to claim. [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing)

**Leadership Type: **
  Matriarchal - Female Dominant. 
-
**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season__: Wet
- __Preferred Nesting Location(s)__: 
> **Gondwa**: Ripple Beach, Snake Gully, White Cliffs
> **Panjura**: Pleasant Hill Haven, Red River, Riverland Peak 
> **Riparia**: Wollemi Forest, Cedrus Forest, Volcano Islands
- __Preferred Skins/Coloration(s)__: Male Lambeosaurus would often have lighter crests, at times matching it to the environment where their nesting grounds were located.  Females remained neutral but, on rare occasions, they may present the complimentary shade of their mates on their crest.  Albino and melanistic variants were consistently avoided, the mutation seen as a sign of weakness and being more prone to attracting carnivores that could endanger young.
> During nesting seasons, elder mares of the herd would often take their pick of the younger and stronger males knowing they could properly protect their young. Elder mares were quite territorial during these nesting seasons but, despite their almost brutish nature, they would be the ones foraging for food and bringing resources to other mothers within their herd.  Lower ranked herd members would often take turns looking after one another’s young, not being committed to just one partner.  To females who were unfortunate enough to be alone during these seasons, especially if they were melanistic or albino, they would stray from the herd and take in other orphaned herbivores that crossed their path.  They would treat these young no differently to how other Lambeosaurus would care for their own hatchlings and created lifelong bonds with their young, even if they were not the same species. 

**Optional Behavior: **
Lambeosaurus are very social dinosaurs and enjoy hanging around other hadrosaurs; they will always seek out a joinable herd.  Being good parents, Lambeosaurus can be found raising and protecting their mixed herds' young.  When claiming territory, Lambeosaurus will prefer grassy plains and areas with plenty of flora for food.  Mostly active during dawn and noon, they can be seen playing with young until lying down for a quick nap in the early afternoons. 
``` ```

profiles.combinedText:
``` ```
> 4 Hours Passive Growth Time
> 4500 Combat Weight

**Lifestyle:**
    Terrestrial

**Group Limit:**
    4 adults, 4 offspring (below sub-adult)

**Herd Tolerance:** 
> Albertaceratops, Anodontosaurus, Barsboldia, Citipati, Edmontosaurus Annectens, Edmontosaurus Regalis, Iguanodon, Nasutoceratops, Muttaburrasaurus, Pachycephalosaurus, Psittacosaurus, Struthiomimus, and Styracosaurus.
> *Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.*

**Mandatory Behaviour:**
○ __Territorial__: When in their **ceratopsian herd's claimed territory**, adult Pachyrhinosaurus must be **vocally aggressive** to other adult sub-apex ceratopsians not in their group.  If after 3 threat calls, the ceratopsians do not attempt to flee, adult Pachyrhinosaurus *may* become **combat aggressive** until the ceratopsians flee the territory or perish. If fearful or unwilling to engage other sub-apex ceratopsians not in their herd, Pachyrhinosaurus **must immediately leave** the territory. 
○ __Brash Babies__: **Adolescent and below** Pachyrhinosaurus *may* be **vocally aggressive** to adult scavengers that are around their group. If after **3 threat calls** the scavengers do not attempt to flee away from the Pachyrhinosaurus or its herd, Pachyrhinosaurus *may* become **combat aggressive** but must not pursue the fleeing scavengers. 
○ __Survivor__: **Adolescent and below** Pachyrhinosaurus **must flee** from adult mid-tier and above carnivores. May defend themself if cornered or exhausted.

**Ceratopsian Combat:**
Ceratopsian herds (those made up of Albertaceratops, Eotriceratops, Nasutoceratops, Pachyrhinosaurus, Pentaceratops, or Styracosaurus) often engage in herbi-on-herbi combat with each other, and may initiate combat __when specifically outlined in their profile mandatory behaviors.__  ﻿Ceratopsians may defend their herd from other ceratopsians.
- Forager-tier herbivores grouped with __only__ Ceratopsians in a mixed herd are still classified as a Ceratopsian Herd. When forager-tier herbivores, such as Camptosaurus, Dryosaurus,  Psittacosaurus, and Struthiomimus are in those herds, the forager-tier dinosaurs **must not** engage in Ceratopsian Herd vs Ceratopsian Herd combat. Instead, the forager-tiers **must flee** from this specific Ceratopsian combat.
- If a Ceratopsian(s) is in a mixed-herd with any low-tier and above herbivore that is not a Ceratopsian(s), they are **not** classified as a Ceratopsian Herd. [*See Pack & Herd Rules 6.(C)(i)*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?usp=sharing)

**Carnivore Aggression:**
- When **solo**, adult Pachyrhinosaurus **must** be **vocally aggressive** towards *approaching* adult mid-tier and below carnivores. If after **3 threat calls**, the carnivores do not attempt to flee away from the Pachyrhinosaurus, Pachyrhinosaurus may become combat aggressive until the carnivores flee the territory or perish.
- When **solo**, adult Pachyrhinosaurus are **fearful of** and **must flee** from adult sub-apex and adult apex carnivores. May defend themself if cornered or exhausted.
- When in a **herd**, adult Pachyrhinosaurus *may* be **vocally aggressive** to adult apex and below carnivores. If after **3 threat calls**, the carnivores do not attempt to flee from Pachyrhinosaurus, Pachyrhinosaurus *may* become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee the territory or perish.
-

**Scavenger Aggression:**
- When in an **adult-only herd**, Pachyrhinosaurus are unbothered by scavengers
- When **solo** or when **adolescent and below** herbivores are present,  adult Pachyrhinosaurus **must** be **vocally aggressive** towards *approaching* scavenger *groups*. If after **3 threat calls**, the scavengers do not attempt to move away or flee from the Pachyrhinosaurus and its herd, Pachyrhinosaurus may become combat aggressive until the scavengers flee.

**Scavenger Moderate Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, may be unbothered. If bothered by the latched on scavengers, they must threat call **two** times or shake **two** times before attacking the scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they **must not** fight back and instead **must flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers **must not** initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*

**Territory Size:**
    Medium - Needs at least 3 adult members to claim. [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing)

**Leadership Type:**
       Leading Bull - Male Dominant.
-

**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season__: Winter
- __Preferred Nesting Location(s)__: 
> **Gondwa:** Mudflats, Ripple Beach, Birchwoods
> **Panjura:** The Mire, Threehorn Grasslands, Travelers Basin
> **Riparia:** Mudflats, Redwoods, Tallbrush Coasts
- __Preferred Skins/Coloration(s)__: Male Pachyrhinosaurus adorned themselves with brightly coloured crests and the same colors would flow down to their backside and tails. Cooler hues, like blues and purples, were popular amongst males and signified they were healthy. These color tones also matched the chilly environments in which they would nest. These bright colors also served as beacons for lost young through thick snow storms.
> During nesting season, males either fought for breeding rights within the herd or left to find a suitable partner to start their own herd. Males attracted a partner by flaunting their nasal bosses. Females would pay attention to battle scars that males possessed on their nose and took notice of the wear and tear of their nasal bosses to deem them as a fit candidate to protect their young. Juvenile Pachyrhinosaurus were notably temperamental and fights between siblings were frequent, and sometimes even dangerous. While head-strong, they were still naive and would often pick fights with other,  sometimes older, Pachyrhinosaurus, which would sometimes lead to their untimely demise. 
-
**Optional Behavior:**
       Pachyrhinosaurus usually sought refuge in tidal flats, floodplains, wooded swamps, and mountainous regions, preferring to travel between these environments to find plenty of ferns and low hanging fruits for food. When attacked or being hunted by carnivorous packs, Pachyrhinosaurus created a circle of protection, ushering their young and sometimes female counterparts within the center as they warded off predators with mock charges and head swings. Due to their instincts to protect members of their ceratopsian species, old, weaker bulls often sacrificed themselves to these packs in dire situations, which allowed the rest of the herd to flee to safety. Fights amongst young bulls were common as these young males attempted to climb the herd's hierarchy, eventually challenging the oldest bull for leadership of the herd. 
``` ```

profiles.combinedText:
``` ```
> 6 Hours Passive Growth Time
> 7800 Combat Weight

**Lifestyle**:
    Terrestrial﻿

**Group Limit**:
    2 adults, 3 offspring (below sub-adult)
﻿
**Herd Tolerance**:
> Amargasaurus, Bonapartenykus, Camptosaurus, Edmontosaurus Regalis, Iguanodon, Kentrosaurus, Lambeosaurus, Lurdusaurus, Miragaia, Muttaburrasaurus, Psittacosaurus, Stegosaurus, Struthiomimus, Tenontosaurus, and Yunnanosaurus.
> *Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.*
﻿
**Mandatory Behaviour**:
○ __Survivor:__ When **Adolescent or below**, Apatosaurus avoids participating in combat with** Apex tier carnivores**, unless attacked first.
○ __Antisocial:__ When at **max same-species herd**, adult Apatosaurus **must** be **vocally aggressive** towards adult apex and above sauropod groups within line of sight. If after 3 threat-calls the Sauropod group does not attempt to flee, Apatosaurus *may* become** combat aggressive** until the herbivores flee the territory or perish.
○ __Aquaphobic__:  Apatosaurus **must not** enter the water once combat has started.

**Carnivore Aggression**:
- Adult Apatosaurus are **unbothered** by sub-apex and below carnivores.
- When **solo**, adult Apatosaurus **must** be **vocally aggressive** towards **adult apex groups**. If after 3 threat calls, the carnivores do not attempt to flee, Apatosaurus *may* become combat aggressive until the carnivores flee.
- When in an **adult-only herd**, Apatosaurus are **unbothered** by all carnivores. If there is an attempt to initiate combat by the carnivore. Apatosaurus may defend themself and their herd until the carnivores flee.

**Scavenger Aggression**:
- Adult Apatosaurus are **unbothered** by all scavengers.
- **Solo** adolescent and below Apatosaurus *may* be **vocally aggressive** towards *approaching** adult scavenger *groups*. If after 3 threat calls the scavengers do not attempt to flee, Apatosaurus *may* become **combat aggressive** until the scavengers flee.

**Scavenger Low Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, may be unbothered. If bothered by the latched on scavengers, they must threat call **three** times or shake **three** times before attacking the scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they **must not** fight back and instead **must flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers **must not** initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*
-

**Territory Size**:
> Large  -  Can claim as a solo adult or group. Territory *must* include freshwater source. [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing) 

**Leadership Type**:
    Independent  -  Will prioritize their own personal survival and benefit before all others.

**Optional Nesting Behavior**:
- __Season__:  Wet
- __Preferred Nesting Location(s)__:
> **Gondwa**: Burned Forest, Hotsprings, Triad Falls
> **Panjura**: Grassland Crater, Lake Pleasant, World’s Edge Falls
> **Riparia**: Black Fern Hills, Redwoods, Tallbrush Coasts
- __Preferred Skin(s) Coloration(s)__: Males would sometimes shed their coats while traveling to nesting locations to match the environment they chose.  Their heads, necks, and spine would have brighter shades while the remainder of their body would stay one consistent tone.  This was a rare sight for males as they usually felt indifferent towards the nesting season as a whole.  Females would remain the same shades with little to no variations, but, on rare occasions, they may attempt to have similar shades to their partner.
> Generally, Apatosaurus didn’t take much time or put much care into nesting as they did not want to bear the responsibility of offspring that struggled to keep up with the herd.  On rare occasions, when two would bond, they would mate for life - not only for the comfort of having a partner but also having someone to assist in battle when the time came.  Females would travel to open areas with large bodies of water or areas with heated pockets of land to keep eggs warm.  Maternal instincts were strong for female Apatosaurus if they bonded with their young and they would carefully build their nests and take their time to bring them branches from berry bushes, so hatchlings wouldn’t have to leave until they were practiced enough to walk without stumbling over their large feet.  Offspring would play amongst themselves and chase their parents' tails, only to tire themselves out and use them as pillows to take their naps.

Optional Behavior:
     The Apatosaurus, despite sharing the appearance of the notably docile Diplodocus, were known for their hot-headed temperament toward not only carnivores, but other herbivores as well. Though generally selfish, within herds with Amargasaurus, they became incredibly defensive and would protect the smaller sauropod with their lives, if necessary.  Apatosaurus were very expressive creatures, especially while migrating with their herds; they would use both verbal and non-verbal forms of communication to lead herdmates towards food and water.  When herded with other sauropods and other small herbivores, like Camptosaurus and Dryosaurus, their tones would shift and they’d even allow these creatures to eat and drink before they did.  This same behavior wouldn’t transfer to other larger packmates however. 
``` ```

rawMessages.author: frostwinterwolf
rawMessages.content:
``` ```
> 6 Hours Passive Growth Time
> 7800 Combat Weight

**Lifestyle**:
    Terrestrial﻿

**Group Limit**:
    2 adults, 3 offspring (below sub-adult)
﻿
**Herd Tolerance**:
> Amargasaurus, Bonapartenykus, Camptosaurus, Edmontosaurus Regalis, Iguanodon, Kentrosaurus, Lambeosaurus, Lurdusaurus, Miragaia, Muttaburrasaurus, Psittacosaurus, Stegosaurus, Struthiomimus, Tenontosaurus, and Yunnanosaurus.
> *Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.*
﻿
**Mandatory Behaviour**:
○ __Survivor:__ When **Adolescent or below**, Apatosaurus avoids participating in combat with** Apex tier carnivores**, unless attacked first.
○ __Antisocial:__ When at **max same-species herd**, adult Apatosaurus **must** be **vocally aggressive** towards adult apex and above sauropod groups within line of sight. If after 3 threat-calls the Sauropod group does not attempt to flee, Apatosaurus *may* become** combat aggressive** until the herbivores flee the territory or perish.
○ __Aquaphobic__:  Apatosaurus **must not** enter the water once combat has started.

**Carnivore Aggression**:
- Adult Apatosaurus are **unbothered** by sub-apex and below carnivores.
- When **solo**, adult Apatosaurus **must** be **vocally aggressive** towards **adult apex groups**. If after 3 threat calls, the carnivores do not attempt to flee, Apatosaurus *may* become combat aggressive until the carnivores flee.
- When in an **adult-only herd**, Apatosaurus are **unbothered** by all carnivores. If there is an attempt to initiate combat by the carnivore. Apatosaurus may defend themself and their herd until the carnivores flee.

**Scavenger Aggression**:
- Adult Apatosaurus are **unbothered** by all scavengers.
- **Solo** adolescent and below Apatosaurus *may* be **vocally aggressive** towards *approaching** adult scavenger *groups*. If after 3 threat calls the scavengers do not attempt to flee, Apatosaurus *may* become **combat aggressive** until the scavengers flee.

**Scavenger Low Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, may be unbothered. If bothered by the latched on scavengers, they must threat call **three** times or shake **three** times before attacking the scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they **must not** fight back and instead **must flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers **must not** initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*
-

rawMessages.author: frostwinterwolf
rawMessages.content:
**Territory Size**:
> Large  -  Can claim as a solo adult or group. Territory *must* include freshwater source. [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing) 

**Leadership Type**:
    Independent  -  Will prioritize their own personal survival and benefit before all others.

**Optional Nesting Behavior**:
- __Season__:  Wet
- __Preferred Nesting Location(s)__:
> **Gondwa**: Burned Forest, Hotsprings, Triad Falls
> **Panjura**: Grassland Crater, Lake Pleasant, World’s Edge Falls
> **Riparia**: Black Fern Hills, Redwoods, Tallbrush Coasts
- __Preferred Skin(s) Coloration(s)__: Males would sometimes shed their coats while traveling to nesting locations to match the environment they chose.  Their heads, necks, and spine would have brighter shades while the remainder of their body would stay one consistent tone.  This was a rare sight for males as they usually felt indifferent towards the nesting season as a whole.  Females would remain the same shades with little to no variations, but, on rare occasions, they may attempt to have similar shades to their partner.
> Generally, Apatosaurus didn’t take much time or put much care into nesting as they did not want to bear the responsibility of offspring that struggled to keep up with the herd.  On rare occasions, when two would bond, they would mate for life - not only for the comfort of having a partner but also having someone to assist in battle when the time came.  Females would travel to open areas with large bodies of water or areas with heated pockets of land to keep eggs warm.  Maternal instincts were strong for female Apatosaurus if they bonded with their young and they would carefully build their nests and take their time to bring them branches from berry bushes, so hatchlings wouldn’t have to leave until they were practiced enough to walk without stumbling over their large feet.  Offspring would play amongst themselves and chase their parents' tails, only to tire themselves out and use them as pillows to take their naps.

Optional Behavior:
     The Apatosaurus, despite sharing the appearance of the notably docile Diplodocus, were known for their hot-headed temperament toward not only carnivores, but other herbivores as well. Though generally selfish, within herds with Amargasaurus, they became incredibly defensive and would protect the smaller sauropod with their lives, if necessary.  Apatosaurus were very expressive creatures, especially while migrating with their herds; they would use both verbal and non-verbal forms of communication to lead herdmates towards food and water.  When herded with other sauropods and other small herbivores, like Camptosaurus and Dryosaurus, their tones would shift and they’d even allow these creatures to eat and drink before they did.  This same behavior wouldn’t transfer to other larger packmates however. 
``` ```

profiles.combinedText:
YUNNANOSAURUS - SUB-APEX / 4 SLOT

``` ```
> 3 Hours & 30 Minutes Passive Growth Time
> 4500 Combat Weight

**Lifestyle:**
    Terrestrial

**Group Limit:** 
    6 adults, 5 offspring (below sub-adult)

**Herd Tolerance:**
> Albertaceratops, Amargasaurus, Ampelosaurus, Apatosaurus, Camptosaurus, Citipati, Dryosaurus, Garrigatitan, Iguanodon, Kentrosaurus, Lambeosaurus, Lurdusaurus, Miragaia, Muttaburrasaurus, Stegosaurus, Struthiomimus, Styracosaurus, and Tenontosaurus.
> *Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.*

-
**Mandatory Behavior: **
○ __Survivor:__ **Adolescent and below** Yunnanosaurus must flee from adult mid-tier and above carnivores. May defend themself if cornered or exhausted.
○ __Tactical Retreat:__ If **solo** Yunnanosaurus is next to a crossable body of water when combat starts against adult apex and above carnivores, they **must** cross that body of water instead of engaging in combat.
- Note: If semi-aquatic or aquatic carnivores are within line of sight or visible in the water, Yunnanosaurus does not have to cross and may fight for their life.
○ __Territorial:__ When in their **claimed territory**, adult Yunnanosaurus **must** be **vocally aggressive** towards adult Yunnanosaurus not in their group. If after 3 threat calls, the other Yunnanosaurus do not attempt to flee, Yunnanosaurus *may* become **combat aggressive** until the other Yunnanosaurus flee the territory or perish.
-
**Carnivore Aggression:**
- Adult Yunnanosaurus must be **vocally aggressive** towards mid-tier and below carnivores. If after 3 threat calls the carnivores do not attempt to flee, Yunnanosaurus *may* become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee the territory or perish.

**Scavenger Aggression:**
- Yunnanosaurus must be **vocally aggressive** towards scavengers. If after three threat calls, the scavengers do not attempt to flee, Yunnanosaurus *may* become **combat aggressive** until the scavengers flee territory or perish.

**Scavenger High Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, they must be **bothered** and must immediately shake-off and **initiate combat** on the latched scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they **must not** fight back and instead **must flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers **must not** initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*

**Territory Size:**
> Medium - Needs at least 3 adult members to claim.  
> Large - Needs 5 adult members to claim. 
> [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing)

**Leadership Type:**
    Patriarchal - Male Dominant. 
-

**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season:__ Wet
- __Preferred Nesting Location(s):__ 
> **Gondwa**: Dried Lake, Broken-Tooth Canyon, Green Valley
> **Panjura**: Deep Lake, Lakeside Meadow, Two Brother’s Hollow, Threehorn Grasslands
> **Riparia**: Coastland Swamp, Dry Fang Canyon, Volcano Islands
- __Preferred Skins/Coloration(s):__ Males adorned themselves in bright, flashy patterns and females were seen with patches of reds, blues, or greens, but mostly had darker colors.
> Brave and tenacious, the male pro-sauropods gathered around in leks to impress the females in a series of show-downs.  These included sound intimidation, kicks, beating their belly, and posing firm with his long back legs.  This process continued until one male remained, granting them the rights to the harem.  The rest of the defeated males were either chased out or forced to become a subordinate – a fate decided upon by the winner.  Females built nests and took care of their offspring, while the lead male protected the herd from any hazard and maintained their dominance from other foreign Yunnanosaurus or young males within the herd.

**Optional Behavior:**
    The Yunnanosaurus are very tenacious dinosaurs and do not like backing down when confronted, even when it may leave their offspring in danger.  They are vocal from the times between dawn and noon as they wander between food source to food source to fill their insatiable appetite.  Male Yunnanosaurus can often be found sporting brighter colors and sparring with other males in the herd for their place in leadership, beating on their stomachs as a form of intimidation. 
``` ```

profiles.combinedText:
``` ```
> 3 Hours Passive Growth Time
> 3500 Combat Weight

**Group Limit**: 
    4 adults, 5 offspring (below sub-adult) 

**Herd Tolerance**: 
> Albertaceratops, Citipati, Edmontosaurus Annectens, Edmontosaurus Regalis, Eotriceratops, Iguanodon, Lambeosaurus, Nasutoceratops, Pachycephalosaurus, Parasaurolophus, Psittacosaurus, Struthiomimus, Styracosaurus, and Tenontosaurus.
> *Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.*

**Mandatory Behavior**: 
○ __Safety in Numbers__: Pentaceratops are wary of  **adult mid tier tyrannosaurids**. Pentaceratops **must not** be vocally aggressive towards adult mid tier tyrannosaurids unless with **3 or more** adult Pentaceratops or in a **mix herd** with an adult sub-apex or above herbivore. May still defend themself if attacked first.
Note: mid tier tyrannosaurids include:  Daspletosaurus, Yutyrannus.
○ __Territorial__: When in a **Pentaceratops-only herd** with **two or more** adults present, adult Pentaceratops **must be** vocally aggressive toward other Pentaceratops not in their group.  If after **3 threat calls**, the other Pentaceratops do not attempt to flee, Pentaceratops **may** become combat aggressive until the other Pentaceratops **flees the territory or perishes**. 
○ __Survivor__: Adolescent and below Pentaceratops **must flee** from adult low-tier and above carnivores. **May** defend themself if cornered or exhausted.
-
**Ceratopsian Combat:**
Ceratopsian herds (those made up of Albertaceratops, Eotriceratops, Nasutoceratops, Pachyrhinosaurus, Pentaceratops, or Styracosaurus) often engage in herbi-on-herbi combat with each other, and may initiate combat __when specifically outlined in their profile mandatory behaviors.__  ﻿Ceratopsians may defend their herd from other ceratopsians.
- Forager-tier herbivores grouped with __only__ Ceratopsians in a mixed herd are still classified as a Ceratopsian Herd. When forager-tier herbivores, such as Camptosaurus, Halszkaraptor, Psittacosaurus, and Struthiomimus are in those herds, the forager-tier dinosaurs **must not** engage in Ceratopsian Herd vs Ceratopsian Herd combat. Instead, the forager-tiers **must flee** from this specific Ceratopsian combat.
- If a Ceratopsian(s) is in a mixed-herd with any low-tier and above herbivore that is not a Ceratopsian(s), they are **not** classified as a Ceratopsian Herd. [*See Pack & Herd Rules 6.(C)(i)*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm)
-

**Carnivore Aggression**: 
- When **solo**, adult Pentaceratops must be **vocally aggressive** towards *approaching* adult mid tier and below carnivores.  If after 3 threat calls the carnivores do not attempt to flee from the Pentaceratops, they *may* become **combat aggressive** until the carnivore flees the territory or perish. 
- When **solo** Pentaceratops are **fearful** of and **must flee** from adult sub-apex and above carnivores. May defend themself if cornered or exhausted, and pursue retreating carnivores until they flee the territory or perish. 
- When **adolescent and below** Pentaceratops are present, adult Pentaceratops **must** be **vocally aggressive** towards *approaching* adult low tier and above carnivores, If after 3 threat calls the carnivores do not attempt to flee from the Pentaceratops or its herd, Pentaceratops *may* become **combat aggressive** until they flee the territory or perish.
- When in a **mixed herd**, adult Pentaceratops **must** be **vocally aggressive** towards adult mid-tier carnivore *groups* and adult sub-apex carnivores.  If after 3 threat calls the carnivores do not attempt to flee from Pentaceratops or it's herd, Pentaceratops *may* become **combat aggressive** until they flee the territory or perish.
- When in a **ceratopsian herd** with atleast 2 adult ceratopsians , adult Pentaceratops are **unbothered** by all carnivores. If attacked, Pentaceratops may defend themself and their herd, and pursue fleeing carnivores until they flee the territory or perish.
-

**Scavenger Aggression**: 
- When **solo** or when **adolescent and below** herbivores present, Pentaceratops **must** be **vocally aggressive** towards scavenger groups. If after **3 threat calls** the scavengers do not attempt to flee from the Pentaceratops or its herd, they *may* become **combat aggressive** until the scavengers flee. 
- When in a **adult-only** herd, Pentaceratops must be **unbothered** by all scavengers. May defend themself if attacked and pursue fleeing scavengers until they flee the territory or perish.

**Scavenger Moderate Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, may be unbothered. If bothered by the latched on scavengers, they must threat call **two** times or shake **two** times before attacking the scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they **must not** fight back and instead **must flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers **must not** initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*

**Territory Size**: 
> Medium - Needs at least 3 members to claim. 
> Large - Needs at least 4 members to claim. 
> [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing)

**Leadership Type**: 
     Matriarchal - Female Dominant. 

**Optional Nesting Behavior**: 
- __Season__: Winter
- __Preferred Nesting Locations__: 
> **Gondwa**: Birchwoods, Hoodoo Expanse, Rockfall Hill 
> **Panjura**: Hoodoo Hills, Threehorn Grasslands, Two Falls Hollow 
> **Riparia**: Twister Forest, Wind Tunnels, Steep Run
- __Preferred Skin(s)/Coloration(s)__: Male Pentaceratops boasted bright, vivid colors on its large crest, sometimes flaunting intricate spots and stripes.  Female Pentaceratops were found adorning darker colored skins with light, neutral shaded crests.
> During nesting seasons, female Pentaceratops would perform mating dances and they used their crests heavily to entice potential partners.  Both genders were very pretentious on the quality of their mate’s crest, making sure frills were well-kempt and free of dirt and grime; scarring was highly sought after as it proved battle capabilities. Having a matching crest with your mate was the highest display of gratitude to the Pentaceratops so, if mates were bonded, they’d sometimes shed their skin to match coloring with one another.  Once offspring had hatched, males would be in charge of watching over young during migrations while females would lead the herd and were charged with finding sources of food.  Younger members were playful but shared their parents' need to remain well groomed, so breaks for bathing together in bodies of water were a bonding activity for a slowly growing Pentaceratops herd. 
-

**Optional Behavior**: 
While within their claimed territory, the female Pentaceratops would spar for dominance of the herd.  Their scars from previous battles garnered great  respect within the herd.  Female Pentaceratops would primarily keep the herd safe from danger, while the male Pentaceratops would provide combat support to the females that took charge in battle.  When not in battle, the herd would gather together for grooming as being clean and dirt free was the priority of the both female & male Pentaceratops, especially as mating season approached.  While the adult Pentaceratops rarely came to the help of their offspring, the children would take up play fighting between each other in an attempt to be combat ready when the need arose.  If there were no other young Pentaceratops within the herd, the youth would pick fights with any of the mixed herd children, no matter their age or gender as both female and male Penatceratops want to be battle ready for adulthood. 
``` ```

*Note: The latched on scavengers **must not** initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*

**Territory Size**: 
> Medium - Needs at least 3 members to claim. 
> Large - Needs at least 4 members to claim. 
> [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing)

**Leadership Type**: 
     Matriarchal - Female Dominant. 

**Optional Nesting Behavior**: 
- __Season__: Winter
- __Preferred Nesting Locations__: 
> **Gondwa**: Birchwoods, Hoodoo Expanse, Rockfall Hill 
> **Panjura**: Hoodoo Hills, Threehorn Grasslands, Two Falls Hollow 
> **Riparia**: Twister Forest, Wind Tunnels, Steep Run
- __Preferred Skin(s)/Coloration(s)__: Male Pentaceratops boasted bright, vivid colors on its large crest, sometimes flaunting intricate spots and stripes.  Female Pentaceratops were found adorning darker colored skins with light, neutral shaded crests.
> During nesting seasons, female Pentaceratops would perform mating dances and they used their crests heavily to entice potential partners.  Both genders were very pretentious on the quality of their mate’s crest, making sure frills were well-kempt and free of dirt and grime; scarring was highly sought after as it proved battle capabilities. Having a matching crest with your mate was the highest display of gratitude to the Pentaceratops so, if mates were bonded, they’d sometimes shed their skin to match coloring with one another.  Once offspring had hatched, males would be in charge of watching over young during migrations while females would lead the herd and were charged with finding sources of food.  Younger members were playful but shared their parents' need to remain well groomed, so breaks for bathing together in bodies of water were a bonding activity for a slowly growing Pentaceratops herd. 
-

rawMessages.author: frostwinterwolf
rawMessages.content:
**Optional Behavior**: 
While within their claimed territory, the female Pentaceratops would spar for dominance of the herd.  Their scars from previous battles garnered great  respect within the herd.  Female Pentaceratops would primarily keep the herd safe from danger, while the male Pentaceratops would provide combat support to the females that took charge in battle.  When not in battle, the herd would gather together for grooming as being clean and dirt free was the priority of the both female & male Pentaceratops, especially as mating season approached.  While the adult Pentaceratops rarely came to the help of their offspring, the children would take up play fighting between each other in an attempt to be combat ready when the need arose.  If there were no other young Pentaceratops within the herd, the youth would pick fights with any of the mixed herd children, no matter their age or gender as both female and male Penatceratops want to be battle ready for adulthood. 
``` ```

profiles.combinedText:
``` ```
> 4 Hours 30 Minutes Passive Growth Time
> 4500 Combat Weight

**Lifestyle**:
    Semi-aquatic

**Group Limit: **
    3 adults, 4 offspring (below sub-adult) 

**Herd Tolerance: **
> Camptosaurus, Citipati, Gigantoraptor, Halszkaraptor, Lurdusaurus, Nasutoceratops, Pachycephalosaurus, Psittacosaurus, Struthiomimus and Suzhousaurus.
> *Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.*
-
**Mandatory Behavior: **
○ __Territorial__: When at **2 or more adults** in a **same-species herd**, Deinocheirus **must** be vocally aggressive toward other adult Deinocheirus not in their group. If after 3 threat calls, the other Deinocheirus does not attempt to flee, Deinocheirus *may* become combat aggressive until other Deinocheirus flee the territory or perish.
○ __Antisocial__: Adult Deinocheirus, when in their **claimed territory**, **must** be vocally aggressive towards mid-tier and above semi-aquatic herbivores not in their group within the territory. If after 3 threat calls, the herbivores do not attempt to flee, Deinocheirus *may* become combat aggressive until the herbivore flees the territory or perish.
○ __Survivor__: Adolescent and below Deinocheirus **must** flee from all adult mid-tier and above carnivores. May defend themself if cornered or exhausted. 
-
**Carnivore Aggression:**
- When **solo**, adult Deinocheirus **must** be vocally aggressive to approaching solo sub-apex and grouped mid-tier and below carnivores.  If after 3 threat calls the carnivores do not attempt to flee, Deinocheirus *may* become combat aggressive and chase fleeing carnivores until they leave the territory or perish.
- When **adolescent and below are present**, Deinocheirus **must** be vocally aggressive to sub-apex and below carnivores. If after 3 threat calls the carnivores do not attempt to flee, Deinocheirus *may* become combat aggressive and chase fleeing carnivores until they leave the territory or perish.
- When in an **adult-only herd**, Deinocheirus **must** be vocally aggressive to approaching mid-tier and above carnivores. If after 3 threat calls the carnivores do not attempt to flee, Deinocheirus *may* become combat aggressive and chase fleeing carnivores until they leave the territory or perish.
-
**Scavenger Aggression:**
- When adolescent and below are present, adult Deinocheirus **must** be vocally aggressive to grouped scavengers. If after 3 threat calls, the scavengers do not attempt to flee, Deinocheirus *may* be combat aggressive until the scavengers flee.
- When in an adult only herd, Deinocheirus are unbothered by scavengers.

**Scavenger Low Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, may be unbothered. If bothered by the latched on scavengers, they must threat call **three** times or shake **three** times before attacking the scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they **must not** fight back and instead **must flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers **must not** initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*
-
**Territory Size:**
> Large - Can claim as a solo adult or Deinocheirus group. 
> May claim lakes, ponds, and marsh - Can claim as a solo adult or Deinocheirus group. 
> [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing)
> 
**Leadership Type: **
  Mated Pair - Dual Leadership. 
-

**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season__: Wet
- __Preferred Nesting Location(s)__: 
> **Gondwa**: Sharptooth Marsh, Birchwoods and Frog Pond.
> **Panjura**: The Mire, Drop-Off Lake, Redwood Basin
- __Preferred Skins/Coloration(s)__: Both males and females kept dull, earthy-toned feathers throughout the year, but during nesting season the males stood out, displaying vibrant colors on their beaks, legs, and arms to attract attention.
> Deinocheirus weren’t just careful planners during nesting season, they were deeply attentive to their partners as well. Males and females performed graceful courtship dances in shallow water, their synchronized movements strengthening the bond between them. Even after the eggs were laid, pairs remained loyal, choosing to stay together through multiple seasons as they raised their young and guided them into independence. Female Deinocheirus were especially protective, often preening both their offspring and their mates as a gentle display of affection.

**Optional Behavior: **
Usually indifferent to most dinosaurs, Deinocheirus nonetheless migrate from one water source to another until they find a location worthy of raising their young. They prefer to travel during rainy weather, but won’t hesitate to abandon a site if they sense danger nearby. Female Deinocheirus are fiercely protective, aggressively driving off any unrelated males or any dinosaur bold enough to approach, the moment they enter the pair’s claimed waters. It’s common to see them along the lake or pond edge, sharpening their vigilance as they preen their feathers and guard what’s theirs.
``` ```

profiles.combinedText:
``` ```
> 1 Hour & 30 Minutes Passive Growth Time
> 1400 Combat Weight

**Lifestyle:**
    Terrestrial

**Group Limit:**
   Unlimited

**Herd Tolerance:**
> Albertaceratops, Amargasaurus, Anodontosaurus, Apatosaurus, Argentinosaurus, Barsboldia, Camptosaurus, Citipati, Deinocheirus, Edmontosaurus Annectens, Edmontosaurus Regalis, Eotriceratops, Garrigatitan, Gigantoraptor, Halszkaraptor, Iguanodon, Kentrosaurus, Lambeosaurus, Lurdusaurus, Miragaia, Muttaburrasaurus, Nasutoceratops, Parasaurolophus, Pachycephalosaurs, Pachyrhinosaurus, Peloroplites, Pentaceratops, Stegosaurus, Styracosaurus, Suzhousaurus, Tenontosaurus, Therizinosaurus, and Yunnanosaurus. 
> *Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.*
-
**Mandatory Behavior:**
○ __Protective Parent:__ With **adolescent or below** maniraptoriformes present, adult Struthiomimus *may* be **vocally aggressive** to all approaching dinosaurs. If after **3 threat calls**, the dinosaurs do not attempt to flee away from Struthiomimus or its group, Struthiomimus *may* become **combat aggressive** until the dinosaurs flee the territory or perish. 
- *Note: Maniraptoriformes include Bonapartenykus, Citipati, Deinocheirus, Gigantoraptor, Struthiomimus, Suzhousaurus, and Therizinosaurus.*
○ __On Alert:__ **Solo** adult Struthiomimus may use alert  (<:AlertCall:1114671294009581569>) or broadcast ( <:Broadcast_Call:1395945878275227732> ) calls to warn other herbivores of approaching carnivores. 
- *Note: Struthiomimus **must** remain separated from any herbivores they are not grouped with, and **must not** third-party in any way (see [Combat Rule 8](https://docs.google.com/document/d/15y-5x9DatxAUlJFKO4S2qMpN1VhAZrtkbpZYtYIs1h0/edit?tab=t.0))*
○ __Survivor__: Adolescent and below Struthiomimus **must flee** from all adult carnivores. May defend themselves if cornered or exhausted.
-
**Carnivore Aggression:**
- Adult Struthiomimus *may* be **vocally aggressive** to *approaching *adult low tier carnivores. If after **3 threat calls**, the carnivores do not attempt to flee away from the Struthiomimus or its herd, Struthiomimus *may* become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee the territory or perish. 
- When in a **same species herd** with **3 or more** adult members, adult Struthiomimus may be **vocally aggressive** to adult mid-tier and below carnivores. If after **3 threat calls**, the carnivores do not attempt to flee away from the Struthiomimus or its herd, Struthiomimus *may* become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee the territory or perish.
-

**Scavenger Aggression:**
- Adult Struthiomimus **must** be **vocally aggressive** to approaching scavenger *groups*. If after **3 threat calls**, the scavengers do not attempt to flee away from Struthiomimus or its group, Struthiomimus *may* become **combat aggressive** until the scavengers flee the territory or perish. 

**Scavenger High Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, they must be **bothered** and must immediately shake-off and **initiate combat** on the latched scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they **must not** fight back and instead **must flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers **must not** initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*

**Territory Size:**
> Nomadic, cannot claim territories when in a Struthiomimus only herd. [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing)
  
**Leadership Type:**
   Swarm Mind - Mimic Behavior/Group Thought. 

**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season:__ Dry
- __Preferred Nesting Location(s):__ 
> **Gondwa**: Burned Forest, Hunter's Thicket, White Cliffs 
> **Panjura**: Grassland Lake, Threehorn Grassland, Two Falls Hollow 
> **Riparia**: Cedrus Forest, Dry Fang Canyon, Wollemi Forest 
- __Preferred Skins/Coloration(s):__  Male Struthiomimus sport vivid, colorful wattles on their throats, which they display by raising their heads and vocalizing. Females are dull and muted, better suited to blending into the foliage.  
> Struthiomimus are often seen as absent-minded creatures. However, they are surprisingly conscientious, caring parents and partners. Both parents take turns incubating their nest, and will defend their offspring with their lives, if necessary. Under high stress, female Struthiomimus sometimes struggled to lay eggs. When this happens, bonded pairs may adopt orphaned young herbivores, caring for them as if they were their own. 

**Optional Behavior:**
    Struthiomimus enjoy feasting on plants, seeds, fruits, and the occasional insect. They are highly social creatures that will hang around other herbivores and serve as an early warning system, emitting shrill alarm calls at the first hint of danger. In exchange, the ornithomimids benefit from the protection of larger species. If threatened, Struthiomimus can also escape would-be predators by running. Their long, muscular legs are perfectly adapted for speed and efficiency. As such, Struthiomimus prefer open grasslands and plains to more closed-off areas. 
``` ```
