profiles.combinedText:
``` ```
> 1 Hour Passive Growth Time
> 900 Combat Weight

**Lifestyle:**
    Terrestrial

**Group Limit:**
  6 adults, 6 offspring (below sub-adult)

**Herd Tolerance:**
> Albertaceratops, Amargasaurus, Anodontosaurus, Apatosaurus, Argentinosaurus, Citipati, Deinocheirus, Eotriceratops, Garrigatitan, Gigantoraptor, Nasutoceratops, Pachyrhinosaurus, Peloroplites, Pentaceratops, Styracosaurus, Suzhousaurus, and Therizinosaurus. 
> *Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.*

**Mandatory Behavior:**
○ __Nomadic__: When **solo** or in a **same-species herd**, Psittacosaurus are **wary and nomadic**, tending to not remain in one location (POI) over a long period of time and traveling across at least 3 POIs before returning to their first/original POI. 
○ __Survivor__: When in a mixed herd, Psittacosaurus will not defend **food** or **water** sources, nor any **territory** regardless of their group/herd composition.  While they stay away from all combat involving territory claiming and resources, if attacked they do defend themselves. 
○ __Fearful Abandonment__: Psittacosaurus are always **fearful of Alioramus and Achillobator**, and must attempt to flee from these predators, unless cornered or exhausted.  Due to their overwhelming terror, they will not defend their herd if under attack from Alioramus or Achillobator. 
○ __Picky Eater__: Psittacosaurus will **NOT** scavenge from player bodies.

-

**Ceratopsian Combat:**
Ceratopsian herds (those made up of Albertaceratops, Eotriceratops, Nasutoceratops, Pachyrhinosaurus, Pentaceratops, or Styracosaurus) often engage in herbi-on-herbi combat with each other, and may initiate combat __when specifically outlined in their profile mandatory behaviors.__  ﻿Ceratopsians may defend their herd from other ceratopsians.
- Forager-tier herbivores grouped with __only__ Ceratopsians in a mixed herd are still classified as a Ceratopsian Herd. When forager-tier herbivores, such as Camptosaurus, Halszkaraptor, Psittacosaurus, and Struthiomimus are in those herds, the forager-tier dinosaurs **must not** engage in Ceratopsian Herd vs Ceratopsian Herd combat. Instead, the forager-tiers **must flee** from this specific Ceratopsian combat.
- If a Ceratopsian(s) is in a mixed-herd with any low-tier and above herbivore that is not a Ceratopsian(s), they are **not** classified as a Ceratopsian Herd. [*See Pack & Herd Rules 6.(C)(i)*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?usp=sharing)
-

**Carnivore Aggression:**
- **Solo** Psittacosaurus of any age will attempt to **flee** from **grouped adult low-tier** and **adult mid tier** (or above) carnivores.  They also attempt to **flee** from **any/all fliers** on sight and will not defend themselves unless grabbed, cornered, or exhausted.  
- **Solo** Psittacosaurus of any age will **avoid** confrontation with **solo** adult low-tier carnivores, but may defend themselves if there is an attempt to initiate combat. 
- When **in a herd**, Psittacosaurus may be **vocal** when they sight any scavenger, or above-listed carnivores, no matter their size or age, to alert the rest of the herd.  They will **avoid confrontation** with adult **sub apex and apex** carnivores, but may defend the herd from mid tier (or below) carnivores. 

**Scavenger Aggression:**
  Moderate - if not threatened.  
  Aggressive - towards **land** scavengers once attacked. 

**Scavenger High Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, must be **bothered** and must immediately shake-off and **initiate combat** on the latched scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they must **not fight** back and instead must **flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers cannot attack/initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*

**Territory Size:**
  Can't claim in a Psittacosaurus-only herd. [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing)

**Leadership Type:**
  Patriarchal - Male Dominant. 

-

**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season:__ Dry or Winter
- __Preferred Nesting Location(s):__ 
> **Gondwa**: Dark Woods, Desolate Pass, Whistling Columns
> **Panjura**: Darkwood Haven, Little Sand Lake, Two Brothers Hollow 
- __Preferred Skins/Coloration(s):__ Leading males would choose to have bright, saturated colors along their faces and quills.  Females tried to match the colors of the area they were in and, more often than not, stuck to natural tones like green, browns, or blacks.
> Psittacosaurus were highly social creatures and made exceptional parental figures to their young.  Females took time and care into gathering small feathers, soft leaves, and dirt, which they placed in a pile to create nests.  While some preferred to lay eggs in intricate tunnel systems underground, many psittaco’s preferred to have large communal nesting areas shared with Citipati, where elders would take turns caring for other members’ kin, which left others free to go on outings to find food.  Juvenile psittacosaurus were inquisitive and mischievous, sometimes play-fighting with siblings, and oftentimes staying outside too long when it became dark.  On occasion, Citipati were kind enough to round up any straggling young and return them to their nests.
-

**Optional Behavior:**
  Psittacosaurus exhibit a unique and remarkable social structure.  These creatures, adorned with feather-like quills in a vivid array of colors, demonstrate a collective sense of empathy, intelligence, and effective communication through distinct vocalizations.  Psittacosaurus family units are characterized by cooperative behavior, with adults actively engaging in the collection of a diverse range of nuts, flowers, and berries.  This provisioning is directed towards both the young members and the elderly or injured individuals within the group.  The creatures showcase a notable level of ingenuity in navigating their environment, employing problem-solving skills to optimize foraging routes and circumvent potential threats.  Among the males, a social hierarchy is established through elaborate displays of color and vocal prowess.  Leadership over family units is quite often contested, leading to confrontations between males, particularly conflicts arising between biological sons and their fathers.  During the nesting season, Psittacosaurus exhibit a pattern of forming weak pair bonds, and with each subsequent season, mates are changed.  This cyclic arrangement facilitates genetic diversity within family units.  The young Psittacosaurus, marked by their exuberance and curiosity, often engage in playful activities within the family setting.  Their vocalizations take the form of games and mock battles, occasionally leading to interactions with adult members.  However, their curiosity, while endearing, exposes them to potential risks such as predation, becoming lost, or inadvertently ingesting harmful plants. 
``` ```

rawMessages.author: frostwinterwolf
rawMessages.content:
``` ```
> 1 Hour Passive Growth Time
> 900 Combat Weight

**Lifestyle:**
    Terrestrial

**Group Limit:**
  6 adults, 6 offspring (below sub-adult)

**Herd Tolerance:**
> Albertaceratops, Amargasaurus, Anodontosaurus, Apatosaurus, Argentinosaurus, Citipati, Deinocheirus, Eotriceratops, Garrigatitan, Gigantoraptor, Nasutoceratops, Pachyrhinosaurus, Peloroplites, Pentaceratops, Styracosaurus, Suzhousaurus, and Therizinosaurus. 
> *Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.*

**Mandatory Behavior:**
○ __Nomadic__: When **solo** or in a **same-species herd**, Psittacosaurus are **wary and nomadic**, tending to not remain in one location (POI) over a long period of time and traveling across at least 3 POIs before returning to their first/original POI. 
○ __Survivor__: When in a mixed herd, Psittacosaurus will not defend **food** or **water** sources, nor any **territory** regardless of their group/herd composition.  While they stay away from all combat involving territory claiming and resources, if attacked they do defend themselves. 
○ __Fearful Abandonment__: Psittacosaurus are always **fearful of Alioramus and Achillobator**, and must attempt to flee from these predators, unless cornered or exhausted.  Due to their overwhelming terror, they will not defend their herd if under attack from Alioramus or Achillobator. 
○ __Picky Eater__: Psittacosaurus will **NOT** scavenge from player bodies.

-

rawMessages.author: frostwinterwolf
rawMessages.content:
**Ceratopsian Combat:**
Ceratopsian herds (those made up of Albertaceratops, Eotriceratops, Nasutoceratops, Pachyrhinosaurus, Pentaceratops, or Styracosaurus) often engage in herbi-on-herbi combat with each other, and may initiate combat __when specifically outlined in their profile mandatory behaviors.__  ﻿Ceratopsians may defend their herd from other ceratopsians.
- Forager-tier herbivores grouped with __only__ Ceratopsians in a mixed herd are still classified as a Ceratopsian Herd. When forager-tier herbivores, such as Camptosaurus, Halszkaraptor, Psittacosaurus, and Struthiomimus are in those herds, the forager-tier dinosaurs **must not** engage in Ceratopsian Herd vs Ceratopsian Herd combat. Instead, the forager-tiers **must flee** from this specific Ceratopsian combat.
- If a Ceratopsian(s) is in a mixed-herd with any low-tier and above herbivore that is not a Ceratopsian(s), they are **not** classified as a Ceratopsian Herd. [*See Pack & Herd Rules 6.(C)(i)*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?usp=sharing)
-

rawMessages.author: frostwinterwolf
rawMessages.content:
**Carnivore Aggression:**
- **Solo** Psittacosaurus of any age will attempt to **flee** from **grouped adult low-tier** and **adult mid tier** (or above) carnivores.  They also attempt to **flee** from **any/all fliers** on sight and will not defend themselves unless grabbed, cornered, or exhausted.  
- **Solo** Psittacosaurus of any age will **avoid** confrontation with **solo** adult low-tier carnivores, but may defend themselves if there is an attempt to initiate combat. 
- When **in a herd**, Psittacosaurus may be **vocal** when they sight any scavenger, or above-listed carnivores, no matter their size or age, to alert the rest of the herd.  They will **avoid confrontation** with adult **sub apex and apex** carnivores, but may defend the herd from mid tier (or below) carnivores. 

**Scavenger Aggression:**
  Moderate - if not threatened.  
  Aggressive - towards **land** scavengers once attacked. 

**Scavenger High Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, must be **bothered** and must immediately shake-off and **initiate combat** on the latched scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they must **not fight** back and instead must **flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers cannot attack/initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*

**Territory Size:**
  Can't claim in a Psittacosaurus-only herd. [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing)

**Leadership Type:**
  Patriarchal - Male Dominant. 

-

rawMessages.content:

- __Preferred Nesting Location(s):__ 
> **Gondwa**: Dark Woods, Desolate Pass, Whistling Columns
> **Panjura**: Darkwood Haven, Little Sand Lake, Two Brothers Hollow 
- __Preferred Skins/Coloration(s):__ Leading males would choose to have bright, saturated colors along their faces and quills.  Females tried to match the colors of the area they were in and, more often than not, stuck to natural tones like green, browns, or blacks.

Juvenile psittacosaurus were inquisitive and mischievous, sometimes play-fighting with siblings, and oftentimes staying outside too long when it became dark.  On occasion, Citipati were kind enough to round up any straggling young and return them to their nests.

profiles.combinedText:
``` ```
> 3 Hours & 30 Minutes Passive Growth Time
> 4000 Combat Weight

**Lifestyle:**
    Terrestrial

**Group Limit:** 
  5 adults, 5 offspring (below sub-adult)

**Herd Tolerance:** 
> Albertaceratops, Amargasaurus, Ampelosaurus, Apatosaurus, Barsboldia, Camptosaurus, Citipati, Edmontosaurus Annectens, Edmontosaurus Regalis, Garrigatitan, Gigantoraptor, Halszkaraptor, Lambeosaurus, Lurdusaurus, Muttaburrasaurus, Nasutoceratops, Pachyrhinosaurus, Parasaurolophus, Pentaceratops, Struthiomimus, Tenontosaurus, and Yunnanosaurus.  
> *Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.*

**Mandatory Behavior:** 
○ __Protective Parent__: Very protective of young and will protect grouped juvies/adols at all costs unless gravely injured [10% HP] and death is likely. 
○ __Discriminate Guardian__: When in a **mixed herd’s claimed territory**, Iguanodon will not participate in a contest battle, but will defend the herd if attacked outside of the contest for the territory. 
○ __Survivor__: When **solo**, adolescent and below Iguanodon must attempt to flee instead of engaging in combat unless cornered or exhausted [15% stamina]. When **adults are present** during combat, adolescent and below Iguanodon must not leave the parent’s line of sight.
-

**Carnivore Aggression:** 
- When **solo**, adult Iguanodon will be **vocally aggressive** to all adult low-tier and mid-tier carnivores no matter their pack size as well as **solo** sub-apex . After 3 threat calls, if the carnivores do not visibly attempt to flee away from the Iguanodon, Iguanodon may become **combat aggressive** and pursue fleeing carnivores until they flee the POI or perish. 
- When **solo**, Iguanodon will be wary of all **grouped** adult sub-apex and adult apex carnivores. They may still defend themselves if there is an attempt to initiate combat via bite, claw, or tail attack. If the carnivores visibly attempts to flee away from the Iguanodon, they **must not** pursue them.
- When in a herd, adult Iguanodon will be **vocally aggressive** to all adult carnivores no matter their pack size. After 3 threat calls, if the carnivores do not visibly attempt to flee away from the Iguanodon or its herd, Iguanodon may become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee the POI or perish.
-

**Scavenger Aggression**
- When **solo** or in an adult only herd, adult Iguanodon will be **unbothered** by all scavengers.
- When **adolescent or below** herbivores are present, adult Iguanodon will be **vocally aggressive** to all adult scavengers. After 3 threat calls, if the scavengers do not visibly attempt to flee away from the Iguanodon or its herd, Iguanodon may become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee the POI or perish.

**Scavenger Low Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, may be unbothered. If bothered by the latched on scavengers, must threat call **three** times or shake **three** times before attacking the scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they must **not fight** back and instead must **flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers cannot attack/initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*

**Territory Size:** 
  Medium - Needs at least 3 adult members to claim. 
  Large - Needs 5 adult members to claim. [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing)

**Leadership Type:** 
  Patriarchal - Male Dominant.
-

**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season__: Winter
- __Preferred Nest Location__: 
> **Gondwa**: Dark Woods, Hunters Thicket, Young Grove
> **Panjura**: Little Brook Meadow, Lakeside Meadow, Little Sand Lake
- __Preferred Skin Colour__: Males stuck with blues and whites to assert dominance by being flashy.  Females tended to blend in with the environment to protect themselves and their kids, which tended to be more brown and black.
> Iguanodons are incredibly protective of their young unless they are gravely injured and close to death, then they will flee the immediate area, even leaving their own children behind. Juvis/Adols always remained near their parents unless they were hiding from a fight, during which they chose to hide in among bushes or outright flee the area.  Bulls were aggressive when it came to courting. They would broadcast, along with stomping their feet, to assert dominance when attempting to court a female.  Female Iggys tended to be docile towards the male, but could become aggressive if the male overstepped.  If either male/female came upon a lone juvi/adol, they would happily accept them into the herd (as long as they were herd tolerant).
-

**Optional Behavior:** 
  Iguanodon are social creatures.  As they travel to resources, they will lovingly call out to passing herbivores and will take in any herbivore juvenile/adolescent that fits within the group.  They enjoy midday and have low activity at dusk and night.  When traveling with children, they will stick to the forests in order for the kids to have a place to hide in case of an attack.  Bulls are more aggressive than females and will protect the herd, while the cows stay with the young.  When attacked, juvi/adol Iguanodons will huddle together to stay safe.  If there are no other children, the juvi/adol will stick close to its parent for protection. 
``` ```

profiles.combinedText:
``` ```
> 4 Hours Passive Growth Time
> 4000 Combat Weight

**Lifestyle:**
    Terrestrial

**Group Limit:**
    3 adults, 4 offspring (below sub-adult)

**Herd Tolerance:**
> Citipati, Deinocheirus, Halszkaraptor, Iguanodon, Lambeosaurus, Lurdusaurus, Muttaburrasaurus, Psittacosaurus, Struthiomimus, Suzhousaurus, Tenontosaurus, and Therizinosaurus.
> *Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.*

**Mandatory Behaviors:**
○ __Antisocial__: When **2 or more** adults are present in a Gigantoraptor-only flock, adult Gigantoraptor **must** be **vocally aggressive** towards all *approaching* adult mid-tier and below herbivores, and other **ungrouped** adult Gigantoraptor.  If after 3 threat calls the herbivores do not attempt to move away or flee from Gigantoraptor and its herd, Gigantoraptor *may* become **combat aggressive** until the herbivores flee.
- *Note: If the Gigantoraptor group is **outnumbered**, or there is an adult apex herbivore present in the opposing group, Gigantoraptor groups are __not__ required to participate in this behavior.*
○ __Tactical Retreat:__   If solo Gigantoraptor is next to a crossable body of water when combat starts against sub-apex and above carnivores, Gigantoraptor **must** cross that body of water instead of engaging in combat.
○ __Survivor__: When adolescent or below, and adult herbivore are present, Gigantoraptor **must** avoid participating in any combat with mid-tier and above dinosaurs. May defend themself if cornered or exhausted.
-

**Carnivore Aggression:**
- When in a herd with no adult apex maniraptoriformes present, adult Gigantoraptor **must** be **vocally aggressive** towards solo sub-apex canrivores, and adult mid-tier and below carnivores. If after **3 threat calls**, the carnivores do not attempt to move away or flee from the Gigantoraptor and its herd, Gigantoraptor *may* become **combat aggressive** and pursue fleeing carnivores until they flee the territory or perish.
- When in a herd with adult apex maniraptoriformes are present, adult Gigantoraptor **must** be **vocally aggressive** to all adult mid-tier and above carnivores.  If after **3 threat calls**, the carnivores do not attempt to move away or flee from the Gigantoraptor and its herd, Gigantoraptor *may* become **combat aggressive** and pursue fleeing carnivores until they flee the territory or perish.
*Note: Apex maniraptoriformes include: Deinocheirus and Therizinosaurus.*
- When **adolescent and below** herbivores are present, adult Gigantoraptor **must** be **vocally aggressive** to all *approaching* adult carnivores.  If after **3 threat calls** the carnivores do not attempt to move away or flee Gigantoraptor and its herd, Gigantoraptor *may* become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee .
-

**Scavenger Aggression:**
- Adult Gigantoraptor **must** be **vocally aggressive** towards all adult scavengers no matter their pack size.  If after three threat calls the scavengers do not attempt to flee from the Gigantoraptor or its herd, Gigantoraptor *may* become **combat aggressive** and pursue fleeing scavengers until they flee.

**Scavenger High Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, they must be **bothered** and must immediately shake-off and **initiate combat** on the latched scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they **must not** fight back and instead **must flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers **must not** initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*

**Territory Size:** 
> Medium - Needs at least 3 adult members to claim. 
> [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit)

**Leadership Type:**
   Independent - Will think of their own personal benefit before others.  
-

**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season__: Winter
- __Preferred Nesting Locations__:
> **Gondwa**: Burned Forest, Stego Mountains, Wilderness Peak
> **Panjura**: Cliffside Retreat, Snake Valley, The Mire
- __Preferred Skins/Coloration(s)__: Male Gigantoraptor molted their feathers and presented flashy colors around the tips of their wings as well as the feathers that covered their face.  Their bills also lightened in shade during nesting seasons, which allowed them to be more recognizable to potential offspring.  Females molted their feathers to match with compatible mating partners but kept the shades as monotone as possible so they could hide in plain sight to protect their eggs.
> During the cold winter season, the sound of clicking beaks could be heard from within the forest accompanied by the small chirps of newly hatched young, who were under the watchful eye of this large avian bird.  The Gigantoraptor was a very attentive parent and the females could be found resting on clutches of eggs as well as freshly hatched young.  Meanwhile, the males were out patrolling the nesting area and warding off any possible threats to their nests.  The females, who were known to usher their hatchlings underneath them to keep them safe, would fiercely defend against anything that wandered too close to their precious nesting grounds.  Males often sparred with one another and showed off their bright coloration in a careful dance hoping to win the eye of a potential mate and being chosen to bring forth new generations into the world.
-

**Optional Behavior:**
   Gigantoraptors wasted little time in finding food and rarely discriminated between sources, eating just about anything they could forage and leaving nothing behind.  They needed constant fuel to stay warm because their feathers were better suited for keeping their eggs warm than providing much insulation for themselves.  The plumage, particularly on their arms, was used for display as well as scaring away would-be egg thieves, or drawing the attention of a potential mate.  These giant birds stayed broody long after their offspring hatched, which caused them to adopt feathered hatchlings from other species that were left behind by their parents, regardless if they are independent.  When Gigantoraptors weren’t raising hatchlings, they sought the company of other avian dinosaurs to satisfy their high social needs.
``` ```

rawMessages.content:
``` ```

> 4000 Combat Weight

**Lifestyle:**
    Terrestrial

**Group Limit:**
    3 adults, 4 offspring (below sub-adult)

**Herd Tolerance:**
> Citipati, Deinocheirus, Halszkaraptor, Iguanodon, Lambeosaurus, Lurdusaurus, Muttaburrasaurus, Psittacosaurus, Struthiomimus, Suzhousaurus, Tenontosaurus, and Therizinosaurus.
> *Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.*

**Mandatory Behaviors:**
○ __Antisocial__: When **2 or more** adults are present in a Gigantoraptor-only flock, adult Gigantoraptor **must** be **vocally aggressive** towards all *approaching* adult mid-tier and below herbivores, and other **ungrouped** adult Gigantoraptor.  If after 3 threat calls the herbivores do not attempt to move away or flee from Gigantoraptor and its herd, Gigantoraptor *may* become **combat aggressive** until the herbivores flee.
- *Note: If the Gigantoraptor group is **outnumbered**, or there is an adult apex herbivore present in the opposing group, Gigantoraptor groups are __not__ required to participate in this behavior.*
○ __Tactical Retreat:__   If solo Gigantoraptor is next to a crossable body of water when combat starts against sub-apex and above carnivores, Gigantoraptor **must** cross that body of water instead of engaging in combat.
○ __Survivor__: When adolescent or below, and adult herbivore are present, Gigantoraptor **must** avoid participating in any combat with mid-tier and above dinosaurs. May defend themself if cornered or exhausted.
-

rawMessages.author: frostwinterwolf
rawMessages.content:
**Carnivore Aggression:**
- When in a herd with no adult apex maniraptoriformes present, adult Gigantoraptor **must** be **vocally aggressive** towards solo sub-apex canrivores, and adult mid-tier and below carnivores. If after **3 threat calls**, the carnivores do not attempt to move away or flee from the Gigantoraptor and its herd, Gigantoraptor *may* become **combat aggressive** and pursue fleeing carnivores until they flee the territory or perish.
- When in a herd with adult apex maniraptoriformes are present, adult Gigantoraptor **must** be **vocally aggressive** to all adult mid-tier and above carnivores.  If after **3 threat calls**, the carnivores do not attempt to move away or flee from the Gigantoraptor and its herd, Gigantoraptor *may* become **combat aggressive** and pursue fleeing carnivores until they flee the territory or perish.
*Note: Apex maniraptoriformes include: Deinocheirus and Therizinosaurus.*
- When **adolescent and below** herbivores are present, adult Gigantoraptor **must** be **vocally aggressive** to all *approaching* adult carnivores.  If after **3 threat calls** the carnivores do not attempt to move away or flee Gigantoraptor and its herd, Gigantoraptor *may* become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee .
-

rawMessages.author: frostwinterwolf
rawMessages.content:
**Scavenger Aggression:**
- Adult Gigantoraptor **must** be **vocally aggressive** towards all adult scavengers no matter their pack size.  If after three threat calls the scavengers do not attempt to flee from the Gigantoraptor or its herd, Gigantoraptor *may* become **combat aggressive** and pursue fleeing scavengers until they flee.

**Scavenger High Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, they must be **bothered** and must immediately shake-off and **initiate combat** on the latched scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they **must not** fight back and instead **must flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers **must not** initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*

**Territory Size:** 
> Medium - Needs at least 3 adult members to claim. 
> [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit)

**Leadership Type:**
   Independent - Will think of their own personal benefit before others.  
-

rawMessages.author: frostwinterwolf
rawMessages.content:
**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season__: Winter
- __Preferred Nesting Locations__:
> **Gondwa**: Burned Forest, Stego Mountains, Wilderness Peak
> **Panjura**: Cliffside Retreat, Snake Valley, The Mire
- __Preferred Skins/Coloration(s)__: Male Gigantoraptor molted their feathers and presented flashy colors around the tips of their wings as well as the feathers that covered their face.  Their bills also lightened in shade during nesting seasons, which allowed them to be more recognizable to potential offspring.  Females molted their feathers to match with compatible mating partners but kept the shades as monotone as possible so they could hide in plain sight to protect their eggs.
> During the cold winter season, the sound of clicking beaks could be heard from within the forest accompanied by the small chirps of newly hatched young, who were under the watchful eye of this large avian bird.  The Gigantoraptor was a very attentive parent and the females could be found resting on clutches of eggs as well as freshly hatched young.  Meanwhile, the males were out patrolling the nesting area and warding off any possible threats to their nests.  The females, who were known to usher their hatchlings underneath them to keep them safe, would fiercely defend against anything that wandered too close to their precious nesting grounds.  Males often sparred with one another and showed off their bright coloration in a careful dance hoping to win the eye of a potential mate and being chosen to bring forth new generations into the world.
-

rawMessages.author: frostwinterwolf
rawMessages.content:
**Optional Behavior:**
   Gigantoraptors wasted little time in finding food and rarely discriminated between sources, eating just about anything they could forage and leaving nothing behind.  They needed constant fuel to stay warm because their feathers were better suited for keeping their eggs warm than providing much insulation for themselves.  The plumage, particularly on their arms, was used for display as well as scaring away would-be egg thieves, or drawing the attention of a potential mate.  These giant birds stayed broody long after their offspring hatched, which caused them to adopt feathered hatchlings from other species that were left behind by their parents, regardless if they are independent.  When Gigantoraptors weren’t raising hatchlings, they sought the company of other avian dinosaurs to satisfy their high social needs.
``` ```

profiles.combinedText:
``` ```
> 1 Hour Passive Growth Time
> 350 Combat Weight

**Lifestyle:**
    Semi-aquatic

**Group Limit:**
    12 adults, 12 Offspring (Below Sub-Adult)

**Herd Tolerance:**
> Barsboldia, Camptosaurus, Citipati, Deinocheirus, Edmontosaurus Regalis, Eotriceratops, Gigantoraptor, Iguanodon, Lurdusaurus, Muttaburrasaurus, Peloroplites, Stegosaurus, Struthiomimus, Suzhousaurus, Therizinosaurus, and Parasaurolophus.
> *Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.*

**Mandatory Behavior:**
○ __Territorial__:  When at 6 adults and in their **claimed pond**, adult Halszkaraptor must be vocally aggressive to other adult Halszkaraptors not in their group.  If after 3 threat calls the other Halszkaraptor do not flee, Halszkaraptor *may* become combat aggressive until the other Halszkaraptor flee from the claimed pond.
○ __Fearful Abandonment__:  Halszkaraptor are **fearful of** and **must flee** from adult mid-tier and above carnivores.  May defend themself if cornered or exhausted.
○ __Survivor__:  Adolescent and below Halszkaraptor **must flee** from all adult carnivores.  May defend themself if cornered or exhauted.
-

**Ceratopsian Combat:**
Ceratopsian herds (those made up of Albertaceratops, Eotriceratops, Nasutoceratops, Pachyrhinosaurus, Pentaceratops, or Styracosaurus) often engage in herbi-on-herbi combat with each other, and may initiate combat __when specifically outlined in their profile mandatory behaviors.__  ﻿Ceratopsians may defend their herd from other ceratopsians.
- Forager-tier herbivores grouped with __only__ Ceratopsians in a mixed herd are still classified as a Ceratopsian Herd. When forager-tier herbivores, such as Camptosaurus, Halszkaraptor, Psittacosaurus, and Struthiomimus are in those herds, the forager-tier dinosaurs **must not** engage in Ceratopsian Herd vs Ceratopsian Herd combat. Instead, the forager-tiers **must flee** from this specific Ceratopsian combat.
- If a Ceratopsian(s) is in a mixed-herd with any low-tier and above herbivore that is not a Ceratopsian(s), they are **not** classified as a Ceratopsian Herd. [*See Pack & Herd Rules 6.(C)(i)*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?usp=sharing)
-

**Carnivore Aggression:**
- When in a Halszkaraptor-only group, adult Halszkaraptor **must be** vocally aggressive towards all unattended juvenile and below carnivores attempting to drink from their pond.  If after 3 threat calls the carnivores do not attempt to flee, Halszkaraptor *may* be combat aggressive until the carnivores flee from the pond.
- When in a mixed herd with adult sub-apex and above herbivores present, adult Halszkaraptor **must be** vocally aggressive to approaching adult low-tier carnivores.  If after 3 threat calls the carnivores do not attempt to flee from Halszkaraptor and its herd, Halszkaraptor *may* become combat aggressive and pursue fleeing carnivores until they flee the POI or perish.
**Scavenger Aggression:**
- When in a group of 4 or more adult Halszkaraptor, they **must be** vocally aggressive towards *adult scavengers*.  After 3 threat calls, if the scavenger(s) do not attempt to flee from Halszkaraptor and its herd, adult Halszkaraptor *may* become combat aggressive and pursue fleeing scavengers until they flee the POI or perish.

**Scavenger High Pounce/Latch Aggression:**
- When pounced on by scavengers, must be **bothered** and must immediately shake-off and **initiate combat** on the latched scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they must **not fight** back and instead must **flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers cannot attack/initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*

**Territory Size:**
       Only able to claim ponds - Needs 6 adult members to claim.

**Leadership Type:**
       Mated Pair - Dual Leadership
-

**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season__: Wet & Winter Season 
- __Preferred Nesting Location(s)__: 
**Gondwa**: Young Grove, White Cliffs Lake, Snake Gully
**Panjura**: The Crook, Little Stone Pond, Crater Pond
- __Preferred Skins/Coloration(s)__: Male Halszkaraptor usually become more colorful during nesting seasons to attract a mate. Female Halszkaraptor usually stick to more neutral colorations to blend in with the surrounding area to prevent predators from finding their nests.
*Description*
Bonding for life, Halszakaraptor will remain with their mate for multiple nesting seasons and prefer to nest on the shore of ponds and lakes. A young Halszkaraptor will follow their mother around wherever she may go, and in turn, mothers will watch over their nest and young until they reach adulthood. Once a mother's male younglings reach sub-adulthood, the leading male Halszkaraptor will often chase out these fresh sub-adult males. The male leader will take on the role of protecting the entire group and often partakes in fighting other males for dominance and to win over other female Halszkaraptor during nesting seasons.
-

**Optional Behavior:**
Adult male Halszkaraptors are aggressive towards other male Halszkaraptor in order to protect it’s family against any potential threats.  Halszkaraptor love to swim in bodies of water and young Halszkaraptor will often be seen playing with their parent(s) or other young in the water.  Halszkaraptor are socially oriented, often performing acts of courtship by bringing their mates or siblings gifts in rocks, fish, shells, or anything else found on the shores of their beaches.  Female Halszkaraptors will hang around each other for company and comfort, taking turns caring for young Halszkaraptor.  During this time, the leading females will correct a young Halszkaraptor’s behaviors, often seen redirecting or even pecking at them to make them stop. 
Young Halszkaraptor life consists of sticking close to their mothers and other young Halszkaraptors. They play in groups and are not often alone. They display different behaviors and personalities from youngling to youngling, however, the most common behavior is crying for their parent(s); often in an attempt for attention when hungry, distressed, or even just for fun.
``` ```

profiles.combinedText:
``` ```
> 2 Hours Passive Growth Time
> 2400 Combat Weight
> Grab: *Up to 651+ CW*

**Lifestyle:** 
    Terrestrial

**Group Limit:**
    5 adults, 6 offspring (below sub-adult)

**Herd Tolerance:**
> Anodontosaurus, Argentinosaurus, Barsboldia, Camptosaurus, Deinocheirus, Edmontosaurus Annectens, Edmontosaurus Regalis, Garrigatitan, Gigantoraptor, Halszkaraptor, Iguanodon, Kentrosaurus, Lambeosaurus, Lurdusaurus, Muttaburrasaurus, Nasutoceratops, Pachycephalosaurus, Pachyrhinosaurus, Parasaurolophus, Peloroplites, Pentaceratops, Psittacosaurus, Styracosaurus, Struthiomimus, Suzhousaurus, Tenontosaurus, Therizinosaurus, and Yunnanosaurus. 
> *Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.*
-

**Mandatory Behavior:**
○ __Loud & Proud__: When in a **mixed herd** and **adolescent and below** are present adult Citipati **must** be **vocally aggressive** toward all **approaching** carnivores. If after 3 threat calls the carnivores do not attempt to flee, Citipati *may* become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee the POI or perish.
○ __Gorger__: Citipati *may* claim any berry bush they are eating from.  If a low or above tier herbivore not in their group starts eating from the bush, Citipati *may* be **vocally aggressive**. After 3 threat calls if the other herbivores do not attempt to flee from the berry bush, Citipati *may* become **combat aggressive** until the herbivore flees from the berry bush.
○ __Decoy__: When in a **Citipati-only** flock and adolescent and below are present, adults **must** attempt to lead danger away or fight until gravely injured and death is likely.

**Carnivore Aggression:**
- When **solo**, Citipati **must** avoid **grouped** low-tier carnivores and **adult** mid-tier or higher carnivores. Citipati *may* defend themselves if attacked, and may pursue the fleeing carnivores until the carnivores flee the territory or perish.
- When **solo**, adult Citipati **must** be **vocally aggressive** to **solo** adult low-tier carnivores. If after 3 threat calls the carnivores do not attempt to flee, Citipati *may* become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee the territory or perish.
- When in a **herd**, Citipati are **vocally aggressive** to all **adult carnivores** but **must not** initiate combat. Citipati may defend the herd from any tier carnivore attacks until the carnivores flee the territory or perish.
-

**Scavenger Aggression:**
   Does Not Tolerate
- When in a **mix herd**, Citipati **must **be **vocally aggressive** toward all scavengers, regardless of pack size.  If after 3 threat calls the scavengers do not attempt to flee, Citipati *may* become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee the POI or perish.

**Scavenger High Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, they must be **bothered** and must immediately shake-off and **initiate combat** on the latched scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they **must not** fight back and instead **must flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers **must not** initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*

**Hunting Behaviour:**
- *When **solo**, or in a **Citipati only herd** running **Scavenger Diet**, Citipati **may hunt** scavengers of any group size within the same territory.  Citipati **must not** pursue scavengers once they flee the territory.* See [Scavenger Species Index](https://discord.com/channels/1028375761930944512/1358607356375601312/1391593370996178984) .
- *Note: In the event of a body down, Citipati **must** claim the body of the **scavengers** they hunted. Citipati **must not** defend bodies and **must** give them up if contested.*
- *When **solo**, or in a **Citipati only herd** running **Scavenger Diet**, may claim **unclaimed** bodies. Citipati **must not** defend bodies and **must** give them up if contested.*
- ***Must** still follow herbivore aggression rules against **low tier and above** carnivores.*
- *When in a **mix herd**, Citipati **must not** run Scavenger Diet, preferring to eat bushes, insects, and flora.*

**Territory Size:**
> Nomadic, cannot claim territories when in a Citipati only herd. [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing)

**Leadership Type:**
   Mated Pair - Dual Leadership. 
-

**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season__: Dry
- __Preferred Nesting Location(s)__: 
> **Gondwa**: Young Grove, Dark Woods, Triad Falls
> **Panjura**: Darkwood Haven, Pinefield Haven, Two Brothers Hollow, Threehorn Grasslands
- __Preferred Skins/Coloration(s)__: Bright and colorful plumage were most common among males.
> Citipati formed social nesting groups amongst themselves, and were even found sharing nesting grounds with  Psittacosaurus.  Males spent the entire nesting season brooding and raising chicks whereas the females foraged for food and led predators away from the nesting site.  When the chicks reached adolescence, they began to venture out of the nest and join the females in foraging for food.

> *When grouped with Psittacosauri, male Citipati will also carefully guard Psittaco offspring. When night falls, and females return to the nest to rest, the males would leave the nest to help gather any stray Psittaco offspring back to the nesting grounds.*
-

**Optional Behavior:**
   Citipati are natural caregivers; caring for their sick and hurt herd members or protecting their young under their plumage.  They enjoy vocalizing during the daylight hours, socializing by singing songs to each other or when looking for other lost members of their herd.  In the evenings, Citipati will splash and bathe in the cool waters of a nearby water source, cleaning their feathers before resting for the night.  Young Citipati tend to be curious and when physically able to leave the nest, will often wander off to explore, requiring their parents to search and carry them back to the nest.  Males will often adorn striking colors, dancing and flashing their vibrant plumage at females in an attempt of courtship; females will sync their dances if they accept.  
``` ```

- When in a **mix herd**, Citipati **must **be **vocally aggressive** toward all scavengers, regardless of pack size.  If after 3 threat calls the scavengers do not attempt to flee, Citipati *may* become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee the POI or perish.

**Scavenger High Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, they must be **bothered** and must immediately shake-off and **initiate combat** on the latched scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they **must not** fight back and instead **must flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers **must not** initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*

**Hunting Behaviour:**
- *When **solo**, or in a **Citipati only herd** running **Scavenger Diet**, Citipati **may hunt** scavengers of any group size within the same territory.  Citipati **must not** pursue scavengers once they flee the territory.* See [Scavenger Species Index](https://discord.com/channels/1028375761930944512/1358607356375601312/1391593370996178984) .
- *Note: In the event of a body down, Citipati **must** claim the body of the **scavengers** they hunted. Citipati **must not** defend bodies and **must** give them up if contested.*
- *When **solo**, or in a **Citipati only herd** running **Scavenger Diet**, may claim **unclaimed** bodies. Citipati **must not** defend bodies and **must** give them up if contested.*
- ***Must** still follow herbivore aggression rules against **low tier and above** carnivores.*
- *When in a **mix herd**, Citipati **must not** run Scavenger Diet, preferring to eat bushes, insects, and flora.*

**Territory Size:**
> Nomadic, cannot claim territories when in a Citipati only herd. [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing)

**Leadership Type:**
   Mated Pair - Dual Leadership. 
-

rawMessages.author: frostwinterwolf
rawMessages.content:
**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season__: Dry
- __Preferred Nesting Location(s)__: 
> **Gondwa**: Young Grove, Dark Woods, Triad Falls
> **Panjura**: Darkwood Haven, Pinefield Haven, Two Brothers Hollow, Threehorn Grasslands
- __Preferred Skins/Coloration(s)__: Bright and colorful plumage were most common among males.
> Citipati formed social nesting groups amongst themselves, and were even found sharing nesting grounds with  Psittacosaurus.  Males spent the entire nesting season brooding and raising chicks whereas the females foraged for food and led predators away from the nesting site.  When the chicks reached adolescence, they began to venture out of the nest and join the females in foraging for food.

> *When grouped with Psittacosauri, male Citipati will also carefully guard Psittaco offspring. When night falls, and females return to the nest to rest, the males would leave the nest to help gather any stray Psittaco offspring back to the nesting grounds.*
-

rawMessages.author: frostwinterwolf
rawMessages.content:
**Optional Behavior:**
   Citipati are natural caregivers; caring for their sick and hurt herd members or protecting their young under their plumage.  They enjoy vocalizing during the daylight hours, socializing by singing songs to each other or when looking for other lost members of their herd.  In the evenings, Citipati will splash and bathe in the cool waters of a nearby water source, cleaning their feathers before resting for the night.  Young Citipati tend to be curious and when physically able to leave the nest, will often wander off to explore, requiring their parents to search and carry them back to the nest.  Males will often adorn striking colors, dancing and flashing their vibrant plumage at females in an attempt of courtship; females will sync their dances if they accept.  
``` ```

profiles.combinedText:
``` ```
> 4 Hours & 30 Minutes Passive Growth Time
> 5500 Combat Weight

**Lifestyle:**
    Terrestrial

**Group Limit:**
    5 adults, 4 offspring (below sub-adult) 

**Herd Tolerance: **
> Camptosaurus, Citipati, Edmontosaurus Regalis, Halszkaraptor, Iguanodon, Lambeosaurus, Lurdusaurus, Muttaburrasaurus, Pachyrhinosaurus, Parasaurolophus, Struthiomimus, and Tenontosaurus. 
> *Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.*

**Mandatory Behavior:**
○ __Territorial__: When in their **claimed territory**, adult Barsboldia *may* be **vocally aggressive** to all adult sub-apex and above hadrosaurs. After 3 threat-calls and/or mock attacks, if the hadrosaurs do not attempt to move away or flee from the Barsboldia or its herd, Barsboldia *may* become **combat aggressive** until the hadrosaurs flee the territory or perish. 
- *Note: Sub-apex and above Hadrosaurs include: Barbsoldia, Edmontosaurus Annectans, Edmontosaurus Regalis, Iguanodon, and Parasaurolophus* 
○ __Survivor__: **Adolescent and below** Barsboldia, when **adult herbivores** are present, **must not** participate in combat between adult dinosaurs and **must attempt to flee** unless they are the main target.
○ __Rivalry__: **Adolescent and below** Barsboldia may claim **one adult** herbivore within their group. If another **adolescent or below** herbivore approaches their *claimed adult*, adolescent and below Barsboldia *may* threat-call or mock attack towards the adolescent or below herbivore. If after 3 threat calls/mock attacks, the adolescent or below herbivore does not move away from the claimed adult, adolescent and below Barsboldia may become **combat aggressive** until the other herbivores flee or perish.
-

**Carnivore Aggression:**
- When **solo** or **2 adult Barbolsdia**, Barsboldia **must** be **vocally aggressive** towards adult sub-apex and below carnivores. After **3 threat calls**, if the carnivores do not attempt to move away or flee from the Barsboldia or its herd, Barsboldia *may* become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee the territory or perish.
- When in a herd of **3 or more adult Barsboldia**, Barsboldia are unbothered by mid-tier and below carnivores, unless there is an attempt to initiate combat by the carnivores’ attacks on any herd member.  
- When in a **mix-herd**, or **adolescent and below hadrosaurs** are present, adult Barsboldia **must** be **vocally aggressive** to all adult carnivores.  If after 3 threat calls a carnivores does not attempt to move away or flee from the Barsboldia or its herd, **adult Barsboldia** may become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee the territory or perish. 

**Scavenger Aggression: **
- When **solo** adult or in an **adult-only herd**, Barsboldia do not perceive scavengers as a potential threat.  
- When **adolsecent and below hadrosaurs** are present, adult Barsboldia must be **vocally aggressive** towards scavenger *groups*. If after 3 threat calls the scavengers do not attempt to flee from Barsboldia and its herd, Barsboldia *may* be **combat aggressive** until the scavengers flee from its herd or perish.
-

**Scavenger Low Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, may be unbothered. If bothered by the latched on scavengers, they must threat call **three** times or shake **three** times before attacking the scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they **must not** fight back and instead **must flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers **must not** initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*

**Territory Size**
> Large - Can claim as a solo adult or Barsboldia group. [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing)

**Leadership Type:**
    Leading Bull - Male Dominant.
-

**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season__: Wet
- __Preferred Nesting Location(s)__: 
> **Gondwa**: Birchwoods, Green Valley, Triad Falls
> **Panjura**: Grassland Lake, Riverland, Threehorn Grassland 
- __Preferred Skins/Coloration(s)__: Outside nesting season, both genders were found marked with dull and dark colors.  During nesting and courtship season, males displayed vibrant color patches across their entire body.  Albino and white colorations were highly discouraged and may be avoided.
> Though docile throughout the year, males would experience a rare temperament change during nesting seasons.  They became aggressive towards other bulls within the herd, butting heads often and picking fights with one another attempting to be seen as a more viable candidate by the females of the herd. Once the eggs had hatched, these new fathers became very protective towards their young and their mate that was now more vulnerable.  Males adorned themselves in bright colors during nesting seasons in an attempt to stand out more, often showing off in large fields and having ritualistic fights.  Victors of these fights would get to mate but the unfortunate loser could potentially face exile from the herd. 

**Optional Behavior: **
  Bull Barsboldia tend to be more vocal amongst themselves than their female counterparts, creating miniature bachelor groups within the herd while cows will watch over and graze with their young.  During the harsh sunlight hours, Barsboldia groups will congregate underneath the shade of trees or bathe in nearby streams to stay cool.  Bulls will battle for dominance over the herd, sometimes exiling the losing bull.  When confronted by a carnivore, cows will put their young first, often matching their pace while a singular bull attempts to lead the carnivore away. 
``` ```

profiles.combinedText:
``` ```
> 2 Hours Passive Growth Time
> 2200 Combat Weight

**Lifestyle:**
     Terrestrial

**Group Limit:  **
  5 adults, 5 offspring (below sub-adult)

**Herd Tolerance: **
> Anodontosaurus, Camptosaurus, Citipati, Eotriceratops, Garrigatitan, Lambeosaurus, Nasutoceratops, Pachycephalosaurus, Pachyrhinosaurus, Peloroplites, Pentaceratops, Psittacosaurus, Struthiomimus, Tenontosaurus, and Yunnanosaurus. 
> *Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.*

**Mandatory Behavior: **
○ __Nemesis__: When in a ceratopsian herd, adult Styracosaurus are **vocally aggressive** toward other ceratopsian herds.  After 3 threat calls, if the ceratopsian herd does not attempt to flee, Styracosaurus **may** become **combat aggressive** until the ceratopsians flees the POI.
○ __Safety in Numbers__: Styracosaurus are **wary** of adult low tier and adult mid tier tyrannosaurids. Styracosaurus **must not** be vocally aggressive towards adult low tier and adult mid tier tyrannosaurids **unless** at max adults or in a mix herd with an adult sub-apex or above tier herbivore. May still defend themself if attacked first.
- *Note: low and mid tier tyrannosaurids include: Alioramus, Daspletosaurus, Nanuqsaurus and Yutyrannus.*
○  __Gorger__: Styracosaurus may claim any berry bush they are eating from.  If a low or above tier herbivore not in their group starts eating from the bush, Styracosaurus may be vocally aggressive. After 3 threat calls if the other herbivores do not attempt to flee from the berry bush, Styracosaurus may become combat aggressive until the herbivore flees from the berry bush.
-

**Ceratopsian Combat:**
Ceratopsian herds (those made up of Albertaceratops, Eotriceratops, Nasutoceratops, Pachyrhinosaurus, Pentaceratops, or Styracosaurus) often engage in herbi-on-herbi combat with each other, and may initiate combat __when specifically outlined in their profile mandatory behaviors.__  ﻿Ceratopsians may defend their herd from other ceratopsians.
- Forager-tier herbivores grouped with __only__ Ceratopsians in a mixed herd are still classified as a Ceratopsian Herd. When forager-tier herbivores, such as Camptosaurus, Halszkaraptor, Psittacosaurus, and Struthiomimus are in those herds, the forager-tier dinosaurs **must not** engage in Ceratopsian Herd vs Ceratopsian Herd combat. Instead, the forager-tiers **must flee** from this specific Ceratopsian combat.
- If a Ceratopsian(s) is in a mixed-herd with any low-tier and above herbivore that is not a Ceratopsian(s), they are **not** classified as a Ceratopsian Herd. [*See Pack & Herd Rules 6.(C)(i)*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?usp=sharing)
-

**Carnivore Aggression:**
- Styracosaurus **must** be vocally aggressive towards adult low and mid tier carnivores.  If after 3 threat calls the carnivores do not attempt to flee away from the Styracosaurus or its herd, the Styracosaurus may become combat aggressive until the carnivore(s) leave the POI. 

**Scavenger Aggression:**
- Styracosaurus must be vocally aggressive to all scavengers, regardless of group size. 

**Scavenger High Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, must be **bothered** and must immediately shake-off and **initiate combat** on the latched scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they must **not fight** back and instead must **flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers cannot attack/initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*

-

**Territory Size:**
  Small - Needs at least 3 adult members to claim. 
  Medium - Needs 5 adult members to claim. [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing)

**Leadership Type: **
  Leading Bull - Male Dominant. 

**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season__: Wet
- __Preferred Nesting Location(s)__: 
> **Gondwa**: Titan's Pass, Triad Falls, Hunters Thicket
> **Panjura**: Threehorn Grasslands, World's Edge Falls, Lakeside Meadow
- __Preferred Skins/Coloration(s)__: Males displayed fluorescent frill colors and females were found with darker coloration to help them blend in with the dense forests.
> When wet season arrived, male Styracosaurus became innately more aggressive toward their male counterparts, shoving other males out of their path or away from food sources, which sometimes resulted in fights breaking out between herd members.  Female Styracosaurus watched with curiosity and interest, but turned away from suitors without a second thought.  Female Styracosaurus tended to be overprotective of their offspring during this time, and did not hesitate to shoved or threaten males, or even other females, away from their newborn offspring to avoid them being trampled by careless herd members.

**Optional Behavior: **
  The leading bull of the herd will chase out young bulls once they reach sub-adult, forcing them to either fight for lead or create their own herds.  Styracosaurus often stick to wooded areas, which they prefer over arid environments with less shrubbery.  Herd members will often fight amongst each other to either teach their young how to fight or to settle internal disputes.  Male Styracosaurus will flaunt their parietal colors and patterns to attract mates or challenge other bulls of the herd. 
``` ```

> 2 Hour Passive Growth Time
> 2700 Combat Weight

**Group Limit:**
   5 adults, 4 offspring (below sub-adult)

**Herd Tolerance:**
    Anodontosaurus, Apatosaurus, Camptosaurus, Citipati, Dryosaurus, Lambeosaurus, Miragaia, Peloroplites, Stegosaurus, Struthiomimus, and Yunnanosaurus. 
*Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.*

**Mandatory Behavior:**
○  __Tactical Retreat__: Adult Kentrosaurus **will not flee/run-away** from any adult carnivore while in-combat **unless out-numbered by 2 or more** or against **adult Apex** carnivores. 
○  __Territorial__: When in their herds' **claimed territory**, or when **juvies/adols are present**, adult Kentrosaurus' will be vocally aggressive to other adult low-tier herbivores who are not in their group when they attempt to eat from the Kentrosaurus’ current berry bush.  After 3 threatening calls, adult Kentrosaurus may become combat aggressive until the adult low-tier herbivore attempts to flee from the bush. 
○ __Family Oriented__ & __Protective Parent__: Protective of juvi/adol Kentrosaurus and often adopt juvi/adol Kentrosaurus if there is space in the group. 
-

**Carnivore Aggression:**
- When in a **herd**, adult Kentrosaurus will do threat displays to passing adult mid-tier and below carnivores by being vocally aggressive and mock attacking.  After 3 mock attacks, Kentrosaurus may attack, but must back down if the carnivore clearly attempts to flee from the herd.  
- When **solo**, Kentrosaurus is fearful of all mid-tier carnivores and above and will attempt to flee unless cornered or pursued.  After successfully defending themselves, solo Kentrosaurus will not chase adult mid-tier or above carnivores after they attempt to flee. 
- When **solo**, adult Kentrosaurus will be vocally aggressive and mock attack adult low-tier carnivores.  After **3 threat calls** or **3 mock attacks**, if the carnivores do not attempt to move away or flee from the Kentrosaurus, they may become **combat aggressive** and chase carnivores out of POI. 

**Scavenger Aggression:**
- Kentrosaurus does not tolerate scavengers; they will be vocally aggressive and use mock attacks to ward off scavengers.

**Scavenger High Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, must be **bothered** and must immediately shake-off and **initiate combat** on the latched scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they must **not fight** back and instead must **flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers cannot attack/initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*

**Territory Size:**
  Small - Needs at least 3 adult members to claim.  
  Medium - Needs 5 adult members to claim. [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing)

**Leadership Type:**
Patriarchal - Male Dominant. 

-

**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season__: Dry
- __Preferred Nesting Locations__:
> **Gondwa**: Savannah, Lower Green Valley, Broken Tooth Canyon
> **Panjura**: GrassLand Lake, LittleBrook Meadow / Great Cascades
- __Preferred Skins/Colorations__: Both sexes chose earthy and green colors to better blend in with their surroundings. 
> Kentrosaurus often came from far and wide to nest in their preferred environments.  Often sleeping in a circle formation with the juveniles in the center.  When there were a lack of predators around, Kentrosaurus would spread out and graze over a large space, often enjoying the rich berries that grow in the dry lands in which they nest.  Kentrosaurus are family oriented and will aid their offspring to the death against predators, often choosing never to back down if their offspring are at risk.  Juvenile and adolescent Kentrosaurus stick to hiding in the brush or under rocks, only emerging at dawn and returning at dusk.

**Optional Behavior:**
Forest dwelling, Kentrosaurus is rarely seen out in open areas unless it's to relax in the sun and rest with group members.  Male Kentrosaurus will sometimes put on displays to attract females or assert dominance in a group.  Due to a strong parental bond, young Kentrosaurus often stick with their family even into adulthood. 
``` ```

profiles.combinedText:
``` ```
> 4 Hours Passive Growth Time
> 4500 Combat Weight

**Lifestyle:**
    Terrestrial

**Group Limit:**
    2 adults, 3 Offspring (Below sub-adult)

**Herd Tolerance:** [only Herbivores]
 Camptosaurus, Citipati, Kentrosaurus, Lambeosaurus, Lurdusaurus, Pachyrhinosaurus, Peloroplites, Pentaceratops, Psittacosaurus, Struthiomimus, and Styracosaurus. 
> *Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.*

**Mandatory Behavior:**
○ __Aquaphobic__: Anodontosaurus **must not** enter water after combat has started. 
○ __Claustrophobic__: Anodontosaurus *may* be **combat aggressive** to **adult low-tier**, and **up to adult apex** herbivores not in their group within **tail swing** range until the herbivores flee. Anodontosaurus **must not** pursue fleeing herbivores.
- Note: Anodontosaurus **must not** purposefully force an herbivore within their trail striking distance in an attempt to initiate combat.
○ __Survivor__: **Adolescent and below** Anodontosaurus **must flee** from adult mid tier and above carnivores. May defend themself if cornered or exhausted.
-

**Carnivore Aggression:**
- **When solo**, adult Anodontosaurus **must** be **vocally aggressive** to adult low-tier and above carnivores. After **3 threat calls**, if the carnivores do not attempt to move away or flee from the Anodontosaurus, they may become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee the territory or perish.
- When **in a herd**, Anodontosaurus are **unbothered** unless a carnivore is within **tail swing range** or there is an attempt to initiate combat on Anodontosaurus or its herd.
- Note: Anodontosaurus **must not** purposefully force a carnivore within their tail striking distance in an attempt to initiate combat.

**Scavenger Aggression:**
- Anodontosaurus do not perceive scavengers as a potential threat.  

**Scavenger Low Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, may be unbothered. If bothered by the latched on scavengers, they must threat call **three** times or shake **three** times before attacking the scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they **must not** fight back and instead **must flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers **must not** initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*
-

**Territory Size:**
> Nomadic, cannot claim territories when in an Anodontosaurus only herd. [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing)

**Leadership Type:**
   Independent - Will think of their own personal benefit before others. 

**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season__: Dry
- __Preferred Nesting Location(s)__: 
> **Gondwa**: Burned Forest, Hunters Thicket, Titans Pass, Young Grove
> **Panjura**: Clubtail Canyon, Tyrant's Gorge, The Highlands, Two Brother's Hollow, Cliffside Retreat
- __Preferred Skins/Coloration(s)__: Males tended to be more green and white, while females stuck to brown and black shades.  Males changed colors to brown/black *if* they were raising a baby on their own. Females that were **uninterested** in mating would change their colors to green/white, mimicking male colors, so as to fool any would-be suitors.
> Males would not try to impress females *unless* they were both located within forests.  When courting, they slammed their tail, shook themselves, and broadcast to show off strength and dominance.  Anos mate for benefit and not for life, so when a partner died, they moved on quickly.  They protected their young fiercely until adolescence, then only protected them if they were in line of sight.
-

**Optional Behavior:**
  When settled down into a claimed territory with a mix-herd, Anodontosaurus will shed its colors to match the new environment, shedding to various greens of the forest or browns and grays to match canyons and deserts.  Mainly day time travelers, they will prefer to travel to new locations at dawn and settle into a territory only during the night, specifically trying to find safe spots to sleep such as in caves or under overhangs.  They will mostly stick to forests as much as possible for food and water sources, avoiding the open and flat sands of the desert and the rocky terrains of the canyons, especially when deciding where to sleep for the night. 
``` ```

``` ```
> 4 Hours Passive Growth Time
> 3900 Combat Weight

**Lifestyle:**
    Terrestrial

**Group Limit:**
    3 adults, 4 Offspring (Below sub-adult)

**Herd Tolerance:**
> Anodontosaurus, Bonapartenykus, Camptosaurus, Citipati, Halszkaraptor, Kentrosaurus, Lambeosaurus, Psittacosaurus, Struthiomimus, and Styracosaurus. 
> *Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.*

**Mandatory Behavior:**
○ __Aquaphobic__: Peloroplites **must not** enter water after combat has started. 
○ __Territorial__: When in their **claimed territory**, adult Peloroplites *may* be **vocally aggressive** to all adult sub-apex ankylosaurs. After 3 threat-calls and/or mock attacks, if the ankylosaurs do not attempt to move away or flee from the Peloroplites or its herd, Peloroplites *may* become **combat aggressive** until the ankylosaurs flee or perish. Peloroplites must not pursue fleeing herbivores.
- *Note: Sub-apex Ankylosaurs include: Anodontosaurus, Peloroplites* 
○ __Survivor__: **Adolescent and below** Peloroplites **must flee** from adult mid tier and above carnivores. May defend themself if cornered or exhausted.

-
**Carnivore Aggression:**
- **When solo** or in a herd **with adolescents or below** present, adult Peloroplites may be **vocally aggressive** to adult low-tier and above carnivores. After **3 threat calls**, if the carnivores do not attempt to move away or flee from the Peloroplites, they may become **combat aggressive** until the carnivores flee the territory or perish.
- When **in a adult-only herd**, Peloroplites are **unbothered** unless a carnivore is within **tail swing range** or there is an attempt to initiate combat on Peloroplites or its herd.

**Scavenger Aggression:**
- Peloroplites do not perceive scavengers as a potential threat.  

**Scavenger Low Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, may be unbothered. If bothered by the latched on scavengers, they must threat call **three** times or shake **three** times before attacking the scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they **must not** fight back and instead **must flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers **must not** initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*
-

**Territory Size:**
> Medium - Needs 2 adult members to claim 
> Large - Needs 3 adult members to claim 
> [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing)

**Leadership Type:**
   Independent - Will think of their own personal benefit before others. 

**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season__: Winter 
- __Preferred Nesting Location(s)__: 
> **Gondwa**: Dark Woods, Hunter's Thicket, Whistling Columns
> **Panjura**: Clubtail Canyon, The Highlands, Two Brother's Hollow 
> **Riparia:** Cedrus Forest, Dry Fang Canyon, Wollemi Forest 
- __Preferred Skins/Coloration(s)__: There is very little difference between males and females, though males tend to be slightly larger. 
> As the seasons change and the weather grows cold, mature male Peloroplites gather in groups to spar. The larger and stronger the male is, the more fights he wins, and the better his chances of catching the eye of a female. While these fights rarely go to the death, any injuries the males sustain can spell disaster in the upcoming winter. Males will often spend time sizing one another up and showing off their power through tail slams and bellows before trading blows, in the attempt to scare away their opponents without risking injury. 
-

**Optional Behavior:**
  Peloroplites, despite their size and fearsome spikes, are a reclusive species, rarely venturing from the forests they call home. Their tough beaks allow them to forage on tough vegetation and roots that other species tend to avoid, which lets them coexist peacefully with most herbivores. Peloroplites also do not seem to mind the presence of smaller carnivores such as Deinonychus or Rhamphorhynchus, and may allow the little carnivores to perch on their backs and pick off parasites. It is only during the nesting season, with young to defend, that these ankylosaurs become aggressive. 
``` ```

profiles.combinedText:
``` ```
> 6 Hours Passive Growth Time
> 5500 Combat Weight

**Lifestyle:**
    Terrestrial

**Group Limit:** 
    3 adults, 5 offspring (below sub-adult)

**Herd Tolerance:**
> Albertaceratops, Camptosaurus, Halszkaraptor, Nasutoceratops, Pachycephalosaurus, Pentaceratops, Psittacosaurus, Struthiomimus, and Styracosaurus.
> *Please see the [Pack & Herd Rules](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.evs5v6kmvhm) for mix-herd limits.*

**Mandatory Behavior:** 
○ __Shield Wall__: When in a **ceratopsian herd** with adolescent and below present, adult Eotriceratops **must not** flee from combat unless gravely injured and death is likely [~15% hp or less].
○ __Territorial__: When in a ceratopsian herd with **2 or more adult Eotriceratops** present, adult Eotriceratops **must** be **vocally aggressive** to other adult apex ceratopsians **not** in their group.  If after 3 threat calls, the herbivore(s) do not attempt to flee, adult Eotriceratops *may* become **combat aggressive** and pursue the herbivore(s) until they flee the territory or perish. If fearful or unwilling to engage other adult apex ceratopsians, they **must immediately** leave the territory.
○ __Survivor__: Adolescent and below Eotriceratops **must flee** from adult sub-apex and above carnivores. May defend themself if cornered or exhausted.
-

**Ceratopsian Combat:**
Ceratopsian herds (those made up of Albertaceratops, Eotriceratops, Nasutoceratops, Pachyrhinosaurus, Pentaceratops, or Styracosaurus) often engage in herbi-on-herbi combat with each other, and may initiate combat __when specifically outlined in their profile mandatory behaviors.__  ﻿Ceratopsians may defend their herd from other ceratopsians.
- Forager-tier herbivores grouped with __only__ Ceratopsians in a mixed herd are still classified as a Ceratopsian Herd. When forager-tier herbivores, such as Camptosaurus, Dryosaurus,  Psittacosaurus, and Struthiomimus are in those herds, the forager-tier dinosaurs **must not** engage in Ceratopsian Herd vs Ceratopsian Herd combat. Instead, the forager-tiers **must flee** from this specific Ceratopsian combat.
- If a Ceratopsian(s) is in a mixed-herd with any low-tier and above herbivore that is not a Ceratopsian(s), they are **not** classified as a Ceratopsian Herd. [*See Pack & Herd Rules 6.(C)(i)*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?usp=sharing)
-

**Carnivore Aggression:**
- When **solo** or **adolescent and below** Eotriceratops are present, adult Eotriceratops may be **vocally aggressive** to all **approaching** carnivores. If after 3 threat calls the carnivores do not attempt to flee from the Eotriceratops and its herd, Eotriceratops may become combat aggressive and pursue the fleeing carnivore until they leave the POI or perish.
- When in an **adult only herd**, adult Eotriceratops may be **vocally aggressive** to **approaching** sub-apex and above carnivores. If after 3 threat calls the carnivores do not attempt to flee from the Eotriceratops and its herd, Eotriceratops may become combat aggressive and pursue the fleeing carnivore until they leave the POI or perish.
-

**Scavenger Aggression:** 
- When **solo** or **adolescent and below** are present, Eotriceratops are vocally aggressive towards grouped scavengers. If after 3 threat calls the scavengers do not attempt to flee from the Eotriceratops and its herd, Eotriceratops may become combat aggressive until they flee from the herd.
- When in an **adult-only herd**, Eotriceratops are unbothered by scavengers.

**Scavenger High Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, they must be **bothered** and must immediately shake-off and **initiate combat** on the latched scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they **must not** fight back and instead **must flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers **must not** initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*

**Territory Size:**
> Nomadic, cannot claim territories when solo or in an Eotriceratops only herd. [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing)

**Leadership Type:**
  Leading Bull - Male Dominant.
-

**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season__: Wet
- __Preferred Nesting Location(s)__: 
> **Gondwa**: Ripple Beach, Young Grove, Green Valley
> **Panjura**: Grassland Lake, Two Falls Hollow, Little Brook Meadow
- __Preferred Skins/Coloration(s)__: Males adorned themselves in bright, eye-catching colors across their entire body, while females matched males in similar patterns, if interested.
> During the wet season, male Eotriceratops took their time to sharpen their horns against the damp rocks as they traveled to their nesting grounds, where they would later participate in hostile fights against other bulls to claim the rights to nest that season with a potential female. Female Eotriceratop, in turn, traveled to these grounds and watched the combat between bulls for a potential partner.  If impressed by a bulls' combat prowess and strength, the female approached them.  They tended to prefer those males who were seasoned fighters, which was made clear by scarring and wear on male’s hides.
-

**Optional Behavior:** 
Eotriceratops form small groups, often consisting of one male and multiple females - such herding behavior comes with their aggressive and grumpy nature, which may lead to fights over resources or even favorite resting spots.  The herd has a hierarchical structure, which is led by a dominant male or a female, depending on their group composition.  Eotriceratops rarely seek help from other herbivorous species, instead relying only on each other due to their massive size and power and may bully smaller herbivores, pushing them away from food or shaded rest areas.  These herbivores are nomadic, which keeps them from overgrazing a single area, as their large group sizes and seemingly bottomless stomachs might easily lead to extinction of local flora if their wanderlust did not keep them moving to new areas.  Eotriceratops are strictly diurnal and will not move from shelter or forested areas during the night as their eyesight is much worse than carnivores that could potentially hunt them in the open plains.

> *Official Eotriceratops and WoTG’s Eotriceratops TLC share the same profile.*
> *Official Eotriceratops and WoTG’s Eotriceratops TLC share the same herd tolerances and may herd together.*
``` ```
