profiles.combinedText:
``` ```
> 2 Hours Passive Growth Time
> 3300 Combat Weight 
> 700 Max Health

**Group Limit** 
       5 Adults, 4 Offspring (Below Sub-Adult) 

**Herd Tolerance**
       Albertaceratops, Camptosaurus, Citipati, Deinocheirus, Eotriceratops, Iguanodon, Lambeosaurus, Pachyrhinosaurus, Parasaurolophus, Pentaceratops, Psittacosaurus, Struthiomimus, and Styracosaurus.

**Mandatory Behavior(s):**
○ __Nemesis__: When in a **ceratopsian only herd** and grouped with an adult apex ceratopsian, adult Nasutoceratops will be **vocally aggressive** towards other ceratopsian herds. If the opposing ceratopsian herd does not attempt to flee after **3 mock attacks**, adult Nasuto may become **combat aggressive** and chase out opposing ceratopsians from their POI. *(low-tier herbivores do not change a ceratopsian-only herd into a non-ceratopsian herd).*
○ __Gorger__: Within a **mix herd’s claimed territory** or in a **ceratopsian only herd with 3 or more adult members**, adult Nasutoceratops may become **vocally aggressive** to mid-tier and below herbivores not in their group, when eating from their claimed berry bush. Adult Nasuto may initiate combat after **3 threat calls are ignored**; will not chase herbivores that clearly attempt to flee. If the herbivore reciprocates combat, adult Nasuto may fight to the death or until the herbivore is chased out of the POI.
○ __Fearful Abandonment__: Due to their overwhelming fear, adult Nasutoceratops will always choose to **flee** from **all adult** large crocodilians (Sarcosuchus and Deinosuchus) Because of this fear, Nasuto **will not protect** offspring if attacked by adult Sarcosuchus or adult Deinosuchus.

**Carnivore Aggression:** 
- **Solo** adult Nasutoceratops will be vocally aggressive towards all adult low-tier and solo mid-tier carnivores.  After 3 threat calls and the carnivores do not attempt to flee, adult Nasuto may become combat aggressive and chase the carnivore out of POI. 
- **Solo** Nasutos will always be **fearful** of adult sub-apex and apex carnivores and attempt to flee unless cornered or pursued.  After successfully defending themselves from adult sub-apex and adult apex carnivores, Nasuto **will not chase** after the carnivores that are attempting to flee. 
- When in a **herd**, adult Nasutoceratops will be **vocally aggressive** to adult low-tier or adult mid-tier carnivores.  After **3 threat calls**, if the carnivores do not attempt to move away or flee from the Nasuto or its herd, they may become **combat aggressive** and chase carnivores out of POI. 
- When in a **mixherd** with an adult sub-apex or apex herbivore or in a herd with **juvi/adol Nasuto present**, adult Nasutoceratops will be **vocally aggressive** to all sub-apex and below carnivores and solo apex carnivores.  After **3 threat calls**, if the carnivores do not attempt to move away or flee from the Nasuto or its herd, they may become combat aggressive and chase carnivores out of the POI.  They will remain wary of grouped apex carnivores, but will defend themselves if there is an attempt to initiate combat by claw, tail, or bite attack.  Nasuto **will not chase** grouped carnivores out of the POI after they attempt to flee.
-

**Ceratopsian Combat:**
Ceratopsian herds (those made up of Albertaceratops, Eotriceratops, Nasutoceratops, or Styracosaurus) often engage in herbi-on-herbi combat with each other __when specifically outlined in their profile mandatory behaviors.__  
- Low-Tier herbivores grouped with __only__ Ceratopsians in a mixed herd are still classified as a Ceratopsian Herd. When low-tier herbivores, such as Camptosaurus, Citipati, Dryosaurus, Pachycephalosaurus, Psittacosaurus, Struthiomimus, and Tenontosaurus are in those herds, the low-tier dinosaurs may **not engage** in Ceratopsian Herd vs Ceratopsian Herd combat. Instead, the low-tiers **must flee** from this specific Ceratopsian combat.
- If a Ceratopsian(s) is in a mixed-herd with any mid-tier+ herbivore that is not a Ceratopsian(s), they are **not** classified as a Ceratopsian Herd. [*See Pack & Herd Rules 6.(C)(i)*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?usp=sharing)

**Scavenger Aggression:**
- Adult Nasutoceratops will be **vocally aggressive** to all adult scavengers, no matter their group size. After **3 threat calls** and the scavengers do not attempt to flee, Nasutoceratops may become **combat aggressive** and chase scavengers out of the current POI.

**Scavenger High Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, must be **bothered** and must immediately shake-off and **initiate combat** on the latched scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they must **not fight** back and instead must **flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers cannot initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*

**Territory Size:**
       Small - Needs at least 3 adult members to claim.
       Medium - Needs at least 5 adult members to claim. [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing)

**Leadership Type:**
       Patriarchal - Male Dominant.

**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season__: Dry
- __Preferred Nesting Location(s)__: 
> **Gondwa**: Stego Mountain, Dried Lake, Rockfall Hill
> **Panjura**: Aperture Pass, The Mire, Swamp Reservoir
- __Preferred Skins/Coloration(s)__: Male frills would have an array of bright colors, contrasting in the environment of their current nesting grounds. Females would adorn light browns and grays during nesting season.
> When dry season arises, male Nasutoceratops become terribly territorial of their female counterparts, becoming hostile to opposing males and brawling for the attention of females. Winning males would later take part in mesmerizing dances to seal the interest of their potential mates.  However, during nesting season, female Nasutoceratops become fiercely protective of their young, threatening any predators near their herd.

**Optional Behavior:**
       Though its build was far different to the likes of the Albertaceratops or Styraco, the Nasutoceratops was a force to be reckoned with. It’s fighting style and stance akin to a bull, they used their stocky builds to brutally push and shove other smaller dinosaurs away from their food sources. Nasutoceratop juvenile’s and adolescents were noticeably food driven, they often rough housed with each other and even bullied weaker members; the only time they’d all behave was in the event that food was abundant. They were mostly brawn however, young members frequently wandered off from packs and even if separated for a moment they struggled to track down the rest of their herd, opting to sit and wait in open fields rather than hiding or practicing any survival skills. Male Nasutoceratops were far more aggressive and territorial than females. Though they had a small tolerance for other ceratopsians their size, if they were not in their herd Nasutoceratops would become very hostile towards any others that attempted to invade their turf. Young sons would fight for the title of alpha even at a young age, more often than not if the elder won younger Nasuto’s would be exiled from their herd and forced to travel in solitude. To prove their strength males would fight one another, using their rock hard crowns like battering rams in a test of strength and endurance, the prize being a female to nest with. Though both male and female Nasutoceratops were known to be brutish, they took pride in having large families. Females typically defended their young more fiercely than males in the event there was a threat towards the herd. Their one true nemesis strong enough to break even the vigor of a Nasuto were crocodilians. Frequent in their marsh homelands, it wasn’t uncommon that young Nasutoceratops would become lunch to a malnourished Sarcosuchus or Deinosuchus.
``` ```

○ __Nemesis__: When in a **ceratopsian only herd** and grouped with an adult apex ceratopsian, adult Nasutoceratops will be **vocally aggressive** towards other ceratopsian herds. If the opposing ceratopsian herd does not attempt to flee after **3 mock attacks**, adult Nasuto may become **combat aggressive** and chase out opposing ceratopsians from their POI. *(low-tier herbivores do not change a ceratopsian-only herd into a non-ceratopsian herd).*
○ __Gorger__: Within a **mix herd’s claimed territory** or in a **ceratopsian only herd with 3 or more adult members**, adult Nasutoceratops may become **vocally aggressive** to mid-tier and below herbivores not in their group, when eating from their claimed berry bush. Adult Nasuto may initiate combat after **3 threat calls are ignored**; will not chase herbivores that clearly attempt to flee. If the herbivore reciprocates combat, adult Nasuto may fight to the death or until the herbivore is chased out of the POI.
○ __Fearful Abandonment__: Due to their overwhelming fear, adult Nasutoceratops will always choose to **flee** from **all adult** large crocodilians (Sarcosuchus and Deinosuchus) Because of this fear, Nasuto **will not protect** offspring if attacked by adult Sarcosuchus or adult Deinosuchus.

**Carnivore Aggression:** 
- **Solo** adult Nasutoceratops will be vocally aggressive towards all adult low-tier and solo mid-tier carnivores.  After 3 threat calls and the carnivores do not attempt to flee, adult Nasuto may become combat aggressive and chase the carnivore out of POI. 
- **Solo** Nasutos will always be **fearful** of adult sub-apex and apex carnivores and attempt to flee unless cornered or pursued.  After successfully defending themselves from adult sub-apex and adult apex carnivores, Nasuto **will not chase** after the carnivores that are attempting to flee. 
- When in a **herd**, adult Nasutoceratops will be **vocally aggressive** to adult low-tier or adult mid-tier carnivores.  After **3 threat calls**, if the carnivores do not attempt to move away or flee from the Nasuto or its herd, they may become **combat aggressive** and chase carnivores out of POI. 
- When in a **mixherd** with an adult sub-apex or apex herbivore or in a herd with **juvi/adol Nasuto present**, adult Nasutoceratops will be **vocally aggressive** to all sub-apex and below carnivores and solo apex carnivores.  After **3 threat calls**, if the carnivores do not attempt to move away or flee from the Nasuto or its herd, they may become combat aggressive and chase carnivores out of the POI.  They will remain wary of grouped apex carnivores, but will defend themselves if there is an attempt to initiate combat by claw, tail, or bite attack.  Nasuto **will not chase** grouped carnivores out of the POI after they attempt to flee.
-

rawMessages.author: cyikez
rawMessages.content:
**Ceratopsian Combat:**
Ceratopsian herds (those made up of Albertaceratops, Eotriceratops, Nasutoceratops, or Styracosaurus) often engage in herbi-on-herbi combat with each other __when specifically outlined in their profile mandatory behaviors.__  
- Low-Tier herbivores grouped with __only__ Ceratopsians in a mixed herd are still classified as a Ceratopsian Herd. When low-tier herbivores, such as Camptosaurus, Citipati, Dryosaurus, Pachycephalosaurus, Psittacosaurus, Struthiomimus, and Tenontosaurus are in those herds, the low-tier dinosaurs may **not engage** in Ceratopsian Herd vs Ceratopsian Herd combat. Instead, the low-tiers **must flee** from this specific Ceratopsian combat.
- If a Ceratopsian(s) is in a mixed-herd with any mid-tier+ herbivore that is not a Ceratopsian(s), they are **not** classified as a Ceratopsian Herd. [*See Pack & Herd Rules 6.(C)(i)*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xw4QL-RWispl_cPW3qCjBFKPsjfrNSfSqHjCJ3WWsP8/edit?usp=sharing)

**Scavenger Aggression:**
- Adult Nasutoceratops will be **vocally aggressive** to all adult scavengers, no matter their group size. After **3 threat calls** and the scavengers do not attempt to flee, Nasutoceratops may become **combat aggressive** and chase scavengers out of the current POI.

**Scavenger High Pounce/Latch Aggression**
- When pounced on by scavengers, must be **bothered** and must immediately shake-off and **initiate combat** on the latched scavengers. In this scenario, if the scavengers cannot hunt this tier of dinosaur they must **not fight** back and instead must **flee**.
*Note: The latched on scavengers cannot initiate combat first if it would break their profile's hunting restrictions/behaviors.*

**Territory Size:**
       Small - Needs at least 3 adult members to claim.
       Medium - Needs at least 5 adult members to claim. [*Please see Territory Rules for mix-herd options.*](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1mnbJnMb6H9FuCYLmMM28ncJT-Ar6sIWk4qCUa89VwgI/edit?usp=sharing)

**Leadership Type:**
       Patriarchal - Male Dominant.

rawMessages.author: cyikez
rawMessages.content:
**Optional Nesting Behavior:**
- __Season__: Dry
- __Preferred Nesting Location(s)__: 
> **Gondwa**: Stego Mountain, Dried Lake, Rockfall Hill
> **Panjura**: Aperture Pass, The Mire, Swamp Reservoir
- __Preferred Skins/Coloration(s)__: Male frills would have an array of bright colors, contrasting in the environment of their current nesting grounds. Females would adorn light browns and grays during nesting season.
> When dry season arises, male Nasutoceratops become terribly territorial of their female counterparts, becoming hostile to opposing males and brawling for the attention of females. Winning males would later take part in mesmerizing dances to seal the interest of their potential mates.  However, during nesting season, female Nasutoceratops become fiercely protective of their young, threatening any predators near their herd.

**Optional Behavior:**
       Though its build was far different to the likes of the Albertaceratops or Styraco, the Nasutoceratops was a force to be reckoned with. It’s fighting style and stance akin to a bull, they used their stocky builds to brutally push and shove other smaller dinosaurs away from their food sources. Nasutoceratop juvenile’s and adolescents were noticeably food driven, they often rough housed with each other and even bullied weaker members; the only time they’d all behave was in the event that food was abundant. They were mostly brawn however, young members frequently wandered off from packs and even if separated for a moment they struggled to track down the rest of their herd, opting to sit and wait in open fields rather than hiding or practicing any survival skills. Male Nasutoceratops were far more aggressive and territorial than females. Though they had a small tolerance for other ceratopsians their size, if they were not in their herd Nasutoceratops would become very hostile towards any others that attempted to invade their turf. Young sons would fight for the title of alpha even at a young age, more often than not if the elder won younger Nasuto’s would be exiled from their herd and forced to travel in solitude. To prove their strength males would fight one another, using their rock hard crowns like battering rams in a test of strength and endurance, the prize being a female to nest with. Though both male and female Nasutoceratops were known to be brutish, they took pride in having large families. Females typically defended their young more fiercely than males in the event there was a threat towards the herd. Their one true nemesis strong enough to break even the vigor of a Nasuto were crocodilians. Frequent in their marsh homelands, it wasn’t uncommon that young Nasutoceratops would become lunch to a malnourished Sarcosuchus or Deinosuchus.
